api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces.ISystemS.title: Interface ISystemStatusProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces.ISystemS.summary: Interface ISystemStatusProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Provides read-only access to the current state of Apple IIe system status and soft switches. public interface ISystemStatusProvider Remarks This interface allows components (such as the UI, debugger, or video renderer) to query the current state of all Apple IIe soft switches and system flags without directly accessing the bus or memory. It provides a clean, observable interface for monitoring system state changes. The provider supports three patterns for accessing status: Direct property access - Query individual switch states synchronously Event-based - Subscribe to Changed event for notifications Reactive (Rx.NET) - Subscribe to Stream for reactive updates All state properties are read-only from the consumer's perspective. The underlying implementation is updated by the bus/soft switch manager through the Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>) method, which is intended for internal use by the emulator core. Properties Current Gets the current system status as an immutable snapshot. SystemStatusSnapshot Current { get; } Property Value SystemStatusSnapshot A SystemStatusSnapshot containing all current soft switch states and system flags. Remarks This property provides a point-in-time snapshot of the entire system state, useful for comparing states, logging, or passing to components that need multiple status values atomically. State80Store Gets the state of the 80STORE soft switch ($C000/$C001). bool State80Store { get; } Property Value bool True if 80STORE is enabled (page 2 redirected to auxiliary memory); false otherwise. Remarks When enabled, page 2 video memory is redirected to auxiliary RAM, enabling 80-column text mode and double hi-res graphics with independent video pages. StateAltCharSet Gets the state of the ALTCHAR soft switch ($C00E/$C00F). bool StateAltCharSet { get; } Property Value bool True if using MouseText character set; false if using standard. Remarks Selects between the standard Apple II character set and the alternate MouseText character set. MouseText provides graphical symbols for building text-based UIs. StateAltZp Gets the state of the ALTZP soft switch ($C008/$C009). bool StateAltZp { get; } Property Value bool True if using auxiliary zero page/stack; false if using main. Remarks Controls whether zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) access auxiliary memory. Allows programs to maintain separate CPU contexts. StateAnn0 Gets the state of annunciator 0 ($C058/$C059). bool StateAnn0 { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 0 is on; false if off. Remarks General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. Some games use annunciators for audio effects or peripheral control. StateAnn1 Gets the state of annunciator 1 ($C05A/$C05B). bool StateAnn1 { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 1 is on; false if off. Remarks General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. StateAnn2 Gets the state of annunciator 2 ($C05C/$C05D). bool StateAnn2 { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 2 is on; false if off. Remarks General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. StateAnn3_DGR Gets the state of annunciator 3 / double hi-res enable ($C05E/$C05F). bool StateAnn3_DGR { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 3 is on; false if off. Remarks Annunciator 3 doubles as the double hi-res (DHR) enable switch. Important: The logic is inverted for DHGR - when this value is false (annunciator off), double hi-res mode is enabled. When this value is true (annunciator on), double hi-res is disabled. StateFlashOn Gets the current flash state for flashing characters. bool StateFlashOn { get; } Property Value bool True if flash characters should be displayed in inverse; false for normal. Remarks The Apple IIe automatically alternates the display of flashing characters (codes $40-$7F) between normal and inverse video at approximately 2 Hz. This property reflects the current phase of that cycle. StateHiRes Gets the state of the HIRES soft switch ($C056/$C057). bool StateHiRes { get; } Property Value bool True if hi-res graphics mode; false if lo-res graphics mode. Remarks In graphics mode (TEXT off), controls whether to display hi-res graphics (280×192 pixels, 6 colors) or lo-res graphics (40×48 color blocks, 16 colors). Has no effect when TEXT mode is enabled. StateHighRead Gets the language card read enable state. bool StateHighRead { get; } Property Value bool True if reading from language card RAM; false if reading from ROM. Remarks Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF access the language card RAM or the built-in ROM. Independent of write enable, allowing read-from-ROM, write-to-RAM configurations. StateHighWrite Gets the language card write enable state. bool StateHighWrite { get; } Property Value bool True if writing to language card RAM is enabled; false if write-protected. Remarks Controls whether writes to the $D000-$FFFF range modify the language card RAM. Write protection is engaged through a two-access sequence to prevent accidental modification of critical code. StateIntCxRom Gets the state of the INTCXROM soft switch ($C006/$C007). bool StateIntCxRom { get; } Property Value bool True if using internal ROM; false if using slot ROMs. Remarks Controls whether the $C100-$CFFF range accesses internal ROM or peripheral card slot ROMs. Typically set by the monitor during initialization. StateMixed Gets the state of the MIXED soft switch ($C052/$C053). bool StateMixed { get; } Property Value bool True if mixed mode enabled; false if full-screen mode. Remarks When true and in graphics mode, displays graphics on the top 20 rows (160 scanlines) and text on the bottom 4 rows (32 scanlines). Commonly used in games to show graphics with a text status line. Has no effect in text mode. StatePage2 Gets the state of the PAGE2 soft switch ($C054/$C055). bool StatePage2 { get; } Property Value bool True if displaying page 2; false if displaying page 1. Remarks Selects which video page is displayed. Page 1 uses $0400-$07FF (text) or $2000-$3FFF (hi-res). Page 2 uses $0800-$0BFF (text) or $4000-$5FFF (hi-res). Can be used for page flipping animation. StatePb0 Gets the state of pushbutton 0 ($C061). bool StatePb0 { get; } Property Value bool True if button 0 is pressed; false if released. Remarks Represents the state of game controller button 0. The high bit of the byte read from $C061 indicates the button state. StatePb1 Gets the state of pushbutton 1 ($C062). bool StatePb1 { get; } Property Value bool True if button 1 is pressed; false if released. Remarks Represents the state of game controller button 1. The high bit of the byte read from $C062 indicates the button state. StatePb2 Gets the state of pushbutton 2 ($C063). bool StatePb2 { get; } Property Value bool True if button 2 is pressed; false if released. Remarks Represents the state of game controller button 2. The high bit of the byte read from $C063 indicates the button state. StatePreWrite Gets the language card pre-write state. bool StatePreWrite { get; } Property Value bool True if the pre-write sequence is active; false otherwise. Remarks The language card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing. The first access sets pre-write mode. A second consecutive access to the same address enables write mode. This is an internal state for the write protection mechanism. StateRamRd Gets the state of the RAMRD soft switch ($C002/$C003). bool StateRamRd { get; } Property Value bool True if reading from auxiliary memory; false if reading from main memory. Remarks Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is used for memory reads. Allows programs to access the Apple IIe's extended 128KB memory space. StateRamWrt Gets the state of the RAMWRT soft switch ($C004/$C005). bool StateRamWrt { get; } Property Value bool True if writing to auxiliary memory; false if writing to main memory. Remarks Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is used for memory writes. Independent from RAMRD, allowing asymmetric read/write configurations. StateShow80Col Gets the state of the 80VID soft switch ($C00C/$C00D). bool StateShow80Col { get; } Property Value bool True if 80-column mode enabled; false if 40-column mode. Remarks Enables 80-column text mode, which uses both main and auxiliary memory to display 80 characters per line (alternating bytes from main and aux). When false, standard 40-column text mode is used. StateSlotC3Rom Gets the state of the SLOTC3ROM soft switch ($C00A/$C00B). bool StateSlotC3Rom { get; } Property Value bool True if using slot C3 ROM; false if using internal ROM. Remarks Controls whether the $C300-$C3FF range (slot 3) uses peripheral card ROM or internal ROM. Independent of INTCXROM. Commonly used for 80-column card firmware. StateTextMode Gets the state of the TEXT soft switch ($C050/$C051). bool StateTextMode { get; } Property Value bool True if text mode; false if graphics mode. Remarks Controls the primary video mode. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays lo-res or hi-res graphics, depending on the HIRES switch. StateUseBank1 Gets the language card bank selection state. bool StateUseBank1 { get; } Property Value bool True if bank 1 is selected; false if bank 2 is selected. Remarks The language card provides two 4KB RAM banks for the $D000-$DFFF address space. Bank 1 is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 is at $D000-$DFFF. Stream Gets an observable stream of system status snapshots. IObservable<SystemStatusSnapshot> Stream { get; } Property Value IObservable<SystemStatusSnapshot> An IObservable<T> that emits snapshots whenever system status changes. Remarks This observable stream provides the same notifications as the Changed event, but in a reactive (Rx.NET) format. Useful for composing reactive pipelines, throttling updates, or combining with other observables. Example usage: statusProvider.Stream .Throttle(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(16)) .ObserveOn(RxApp.MainThreadScheduler) .Subscribe(snapshot => UpdateUI(snapshot)); Methods Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>) Internal mutation method for updating the status snapshot. void Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder> mutator) Parameters mutator Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder> A builder action that modifies the snapshot state Remarks For internal use by emulator core only. This method is called by the bus or soft switch manager to update the system status when switches are toggled. It uses a builder pattern to efficiently update the immutable snapshot and notify subscribers. Consumers should not call this method - use the read-only properties and subscribe to Changed or Stream instead. Events Changed Raised when any system status changes. event EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>? Changed Event Type EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot> Remarks This event fires whenever any soft switch or system flag changes state. The event args contain a SystemStatusSnapshot with the new state. Subscribers can use this for event-driven updates to UI or other components. For reactive programming patterns, consider using Stream instead. MemoryMappingChanged Event raised when any soft switch affecting memory mapping changes. event EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>? MemoryMappingChanged Event Type EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot> Remarks Fires only when RAMRD, RAMWRT, ALTZP, 80STORE, HIRES, PAGE2, INTCXROM, SLOTC3ROM, HIGHWRITE, BANK1, or HIGHREAD change. Provides the full SystemStatusSnapshot for convenience.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces.SlotNumb.title: Enum SlotNumber | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces.SlotNumb.summary: Enum SlotNumber Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Defines the slot numbers for Apple IIe expansion cards. public enum SlotNumber Fields Slot1 = 0 Expansion slot 1. Slot2 = 1 Expansion slot 2. Slot3 = 2 Expansion slot 3 (typically 80-column card). Slot4 = 3 Expansion slot 4. Slot5 = 4 Expansion slot 5. Slot6 = 5 Expansion slot 6 (typically disk controller). Slot7 = 6 Expansion slot 7. Remarks The Apple IIe provides seven expansion slots numbered 1 through 7. Slot 0 is reserved for system use and is not accessible to expansion cards. Each slot provides access to three address spaces: I/O ($C0x0-$C0xF), ROM ($Cx00-$CxFF), and shared extended ROM ($C800-$CFFF).

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.LanguageCard.html.title: Class LanguageCard | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.LanguageCard.html.summary: Class LanguageCard Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Manages the Apple IIe Language Card memory banking for the $D000-$FFFF address space. public class LanguageCard : ILanguageCard, IMemory Inheritance object LanguageCard Implements ILanguageCard IMemory Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Implements the Language Card banking mechanism that allows 16KB of RAM to be mapped into the normally ROM-occupied address space ($D000-$FFFF), with two switchable 4KB banks for $D000-$DFFF and a shared 8KB region for $E000-$FFFF. Memory Layout: Apple IIe Address Space: $D000-$DFFF (4KB) - Bank 1 or Bank 2 (switchable) $E000-$FFFF (8KB) - Common area (shared between banks) Physical RAM Layout (16KB total): Bank 1: $0000-$0FFF (4KB) + $2000-$3FFF (8KB) = 12KB Bank 2: $1000-$3FFF (12KB) Banking Control: The Language Card reads state from ISystemStatusProvider to determine: StateHighRead - Read from RAM vs ROM StateHighWrite - RAM write enable StateUseBank1 - Bank 1 vs Bank 2 selection StateRamRd - Main vs auxiliary memory for reads StateRamWrt - Main vs auxiliary memory for writes Design Philosophy: This class is a pure memory accessor that queries current state flags but does not manage state transitions. State management is handled by the soft switch controller, which updates the ISystemStatusProvider. This separation provides clean architecture, optimal performance, and excellent testability. Performance: All read/write methods use aggressive inlining for maximum performance in this hot path (called hundreds of thousands of times per second). Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Assumes single-threaded CPU execution. The ISystemStatusProvider should handle any necessary synchronization. Constructors LanguageCard(ISystemRam, ISystemRam?, ISystemRomProvider, IFloatingBusProvider, ISystemStatusProvider) Manages the Apple IIe Language Card memory banking for the $D000-$FFFF address space. public LanguageCard(ISystemRam mainRam, ISystemRam? auxRam, ISystemRomProvider systemRom, IFloatingBusProvider floatingBus, ISystemStatusProvider status) Parameters mainRam ISystemRam auxRam ISystemRam systemRom ISystemRomProvider floatingBus IFloatingBusProvider status ISystemStatusProvider Remarks Purpose: Implements the Language Card banking mechanism that allows 16KB of RAM to be mapped into the normally ROM-occupied address space ($D000-$FFFF), with two switchable 4KB banks for $D000-$DFFF and a shared 8KB region for $E000-$FFFF. Memory Layout: Apple IIe Address Space: $D000-$DFFF (4KB) - Bank 1 or Bank 2 (switchable) $E000-$FFFF (8KB) - Common area (shared between banks) Physical RAM Layout (16KB total): Bank 1: $0000-$0FFF (4KB) + $2000-$3FFF (8KB) = 12KB Bank 2: $1000-$3FFF (12KB) Banking Control: The Language Card reads state from ISystemStatusProvider to determine: StateHighRead - Read from RAM vs ROM StateHighWrite - RAM write enable StateUseBank1 - Bank 1 vs Bank 2 selection StateRamRd - Main vs auxiliary memory for reads StateRamWrt - Main vs auxiliary memory for writes Design Philosophy: This class is a pure memory accessor that queries current state flags but does not manage state transitions. State management is handled by the soft switch controller, which updates the ISystemStatusProvider. This separation provides clean architecture, optimal performance, and excellent testability. Performance: All read/write methods use aggressive inlining for maximum performance in this hot path (called hundreds of thousands of times per second). Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Assumes single-threaded CPU execution. The ISystemStatusProvider should handle any necessary synchronization. Properties this[ushort] Gets or sets a byte at the specified address using array-like indexer syntax. public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; } Parameters address ushort Address to access, typically in the $D000-$FFFF range. Property Value byte Byte value from RAM, ROM, or floating bus. Remarks Convenience Syntax: Provides array-like access to the Language Card: byte value = languageCard[0xD000]; // Read languageCard[0xE000] = 0x42; // Write Implementation: Getter delegates to Read(ushort), setter delegates to Write(ushort, byte). See those methods for detailed behavior. Size Gets the size of the Language Card address space (12KB). public int Size { get; } Property Value int Always returns 0x3000 (12,288 bytes), representing the $D000-$FFFF address range. Remarks Note: The Language Card uses 16KB of physical RAM, but only 12KB is addressable at any given time due to bank switching. The returned size matches the accessible address space ($D000-$FFFF). Methods Read(ushort) Reads a byte from the Language Card address space. public byte Read(ushort address) Parameters address ushort Address to read from, typically in the $D000-$FFFF range (though any 16-bit address is accepted for calculation purposes). Returns byte Byte value from RAM, ROM, or floating bus, depending on system state. Remarks Read Decision Logic: If StateHighRead is true: If StateRamRd is true: Read from auxiliary RAM (or floating bus if aux doesn't exist) Else: Read from main RAM Else: Read from system ROM Floating Bus: If auxiliary memory is accessed but doesn't exist, returns the floating bus value instead. This models Apple IIe hardware behavior where unmapped memory reads return the last value on the data bus. Bank Selection: The StateUseBank1 flag determines which 4KB bank is accessed for $D000-$DFFF. The $E000-$FFFF region is shared. Performance: Aggressively inlined. Read from main RAM with ROM fallback is the common path and executes in ~7-10 cycles after inlining. Write(ushort, byte) Writes a byte to the Language Card address space. public void Write(ushort address, byte data) Parameters address ushort Address to write to, typically in the $D000-$FFFF range (though any 16-bit address is accepted for calculation purposes). data byte Byte value to write. Remarks Write Protection: Writes are only executed if StateHighWrite is true. The Language Card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing, preventing accidental ROM overwrites. Write Decision Logic: If StateHighWrite is false: Return (no-op, ROM is write-protected) If StateRamWrt is true: Write to auxiliary RAM (or ignore if aux doesn't exist) Else: Write to main RAM Bank Selection: The StateUseBank1 flag determines which 4KB bank is written to for $D000-$DFFF. The $E000-$FFFF region is shared. ROM Write Attempts: Writes when StateHighWrite is false are silently ignored (no-op), matching Apple IIe hardware behavior where ROM writes have no effect. Performance: Aggressively inlined. Write protection check is the fast path (early return), keeping overhead minimal.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.MemoryPool.html.summary: Class MemoryPool Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll public sealed class MemoryPool : IMemory, IMemoryAccessNotifier, IDirectMemoryPoolReader, IDisposable Inheritance object MemoryPool Implements IMemory IMemoryAccessNotifier IDirectMemoryPoolReader IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors MemoryPool(ISystemStatusProvider, ILanguageCard, ISystemRamSelector, int, bool) public MemoryPool(ISystemStatusProvider status, ILanguageCard langCard, ISystemRamSelector systemRam, int poolSize = 163584, bool randomInit = false) Parameters status ISystemStatusProvider langCard ILanguageCard systemRam ISystemRamSelector poolSize int randomInit bool Properties this[ushort] Gets or sets a byte at the specified address (indexer syntax). public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; } Parameters address ushort 16-bit address ($0000-$FFFF). Property Value byte Byte value at the mapped physical location. Size Gets the size of the addressable memory space (always 64KB for 6502). public int Size { get; } Property Value int 65,536 bytes ($0000-$FFFF). Remarks The 6502 has a 16-bit address bus, providing 64KB of address space. The actual physical memory may be larger (128KB main+aux + ROM), but it's accessed through this 64KB window via soft switch-controlled bank switching. Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() InstallApple2ROM(byte[]) public void InstallApple2ROM(byte[] rom) Parameters rom byte[] Read(ushort) Reads a byte from the specified address in the mapped memory space. public byte Read(ushort address) Parameters address ushort 16-bit address to read from ($0000-$FFFF). Returns byte Byte value at the mapped physical location for this address. Remarks The physical memory accessed depends on current soft switch states (RAMRD, ALTZP, etc.). This method delegates to ReadMapped(ushort) which performs the region-based lookup. ReadMapped(ushort) public byte ReadMapped(ushort address) Parameters address ushort Returns byte ReadPool(int) public byte ReadPool(int address) Parameters address int Returns byte ReadRawAux(int) Reads directly from the auxiliary memory bank, bypassing soft switch mapping. public byte ReadRawAux(int address) Parameters address int Physical address in the pool (0-65535 for aux bank). Returns byte Byte value at the physical location. Remarks Direct Access: Reads from the auxiliary 64KB bank (offset 0x10000 in the pool). Used for 80-column display, double hi-res graphics, and debugging. Example: 80-column text mode interleaves main and auxiliary memory for each character position, so the renderer uses this method to access aux memory directly. ReadRawMain(int) Reads directly from the main memory bank, bypassing soft switch mapping. public byte ReadRawMain(int address) Parameters address int Physical address in the pool (0-65535 for main bank). Returns byte Byte value at the physical location. Remarks Direct Access: This method bypasses the soft switch mapping system and reads directly from the physical main memory bank. Used by debuggers, memory viewers, and video renderers that need to see actual RAM contents regardless of current bank switching. Example: The video renderer needs to read from hi-res page 2 even if PAGE2 is off, so it uses this method instead of the normal Read(ushort) method. Remarks The physical memory accessed depends on current soft switch states (RAMWRT, ALTZP, etc.). Write-protected regions (ROM, unmapped I/O) are silently ignored. api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.NullCard.html.title: Class NullCard | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.NullCard.html.summary: Class NullCard Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Represents an empty expansion slot in the Apple IIe, providing no functionality. public class NullCard : ICard, IConfigurable Inheritance object NullCard Implements ICard IConfigurable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The NullCard is a special implementation of ICard that represents the absence of a peripheral card. It is the only card type guaranteed to be available in the emulator and serves as the default state for all expansion slots upon initialization. Purpose and Design: Default State: All slots (1-7) are initialized with NullCard instances, representing empty slots Removal Placeholder: When a card is removed via RemoveCard(SlotNumber), it's replaced with a NullCard Floating Bus Enabler: By returning null for all read operations, NullCard causes the slots system to return floating bus values Silent Writes: All write operations are no-ops, preventing any state changes or side effects Behavioral Characteristics: The NullCard implements complete passivity: All read methods return null (indicating "no response") All write methods perform no operation No internal state is maintained (completely stateless) Cloning creates a new instance (though functionally identical) Configuration methods are no-ops (GetMetadata() returns empty, ApplyMetadata(string) always succeeds) Configuration Support: NullCard implements IConfigurable (via ICard) with trivial behavior: GetMetadata() always returns an empty string, and ApplyMetadata(string) always returns true while ignoring any provided metadata. This allows NullCard to participate in configuration serialization without requiring special-case handling. Real Hardware Analogy: The NullCard emulates the behavior of an actual Apple IIe with no card installed in a slot. When software accesses an empty slot's I/O or ROM space, the hardware returns whatever data happens to be floating on the bus at that moment, which this implementation delegates to the IFloatingBusProvider. Factory Integration: The ICardFactory must always be able to provide NullCard instances via GetNullCard(). This is enforced during Slots initialization and card removal operations, which throw exceptions if NullCard creation fails. Thread Safety: The NullCard is completely stateless and therefore inherently thread-safe. Multiple threads can safely call any methods simultaneously without synchronization, though normal emulator usage restricts all card operations to the emulator worker thread. Properties Description Gets a brief description of the NullCard. public string Description { get; } Property Value string Always returns "No card". Remarks This minimal description is appropriate since the NullCard represents the absence of functionality rather than any actual peripheral device. Id Gets the unique identifier for the NullCard type. public int Id { get; } Property Value int Always returns 0. Remarks The ID value of 0 is reserved for NullCard and serves as a sentinel value throughout the emulator. Code can check card.Id == 0 to determine if a slot is empty, which is formalized in the IsEmpty(SlotNumber) convenience method. All other card types must use IDs ≥ 1 to avoid conflicts. The ICardFactory validates that no registered card uses ID 0 during registration. Name Gets the display name for empty slots. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Always returns "Empty Slot". Remarks This name appears in UI elements like slot configuration dialogs and status displays to indicate that no peripheral card is installed. Methods ApplyMetadata(string) Applies configuration metadata to this NullCard. public bool ApplyMetadata(string metadata) Parameters metadata string The configuration metadata (ignored). Returns bool Always returns true since NullCard accepts any metadata without effect. Remarks NullCard is completely stateless and cannot be configured. This method ignores the provided metadata and always returns true to indicate "success" in the sense that the NullCard remains in its expected state (no configuration). This implementation allows NullCard to participate in configuration restoration without requiring special-case handling. Empty slots are simply skipped during configuration, and any metadata provided is silently ignored. Clone() Creates a new instance of NullCard. public ICard Clone() Returns ICard A new NullCard instance. Remarks Although NullCard is stateless and all instances are functionally identical, the Clone() method creates a new instance to maintain consistency with the ICard contract and allow each slot to have its own card reference. This is used during Slots initialization to populate all slots with independent NullCard instances, even though they behave identically. GetMetadata() Gets the configuration metadata for this NullCard. public string GetMetadata() Returns string Always returns an empty string since NullCard has no configurable state. Remarks NullCard is completely stateless and has no configuration to persist. This method always returns an empty string to indicate the absence of any configuration data. This implementation satisfies the IConfigurable contract inherited through ICard, allowing NullCard to participate in configuration serialization without requiring special-case handling. ReadExtendedRom(ushort) Attempts to read from the card's extended ROM space, always returning null. public byte? ReadExtendedRom(ushort _) Parameters _ ushort The extended ROM offset (ignored). Returns byte? Always null, indicating no response. Remarks The null return causes ISlots to return a floating bus value. Even when an empty slot's extended ROM is "selected" via BankSelect, reads return floating bus values since no physical ROM is present. ReadIO(byte) Attempts to read from the card's I/O space, always returning null. public byte? ReadIO(byte _) Parameters _ byte The I/O offset (ignored). Returns byte? Always null, indicating no response. Remarks The null return causes ISlots to return a floating bus value, emulating the behavior of reading from an empty slot on real Apple IIe hardware. ReadRom(byte) Attempts to read from the card's ROM space, always returning null. public byte? ReadRom(byte _) Parameters _ byte The ROM offset (ignored). Returns byte? Always null, indicating no response. Remarks The null return causes ISlots to return a floating bus value. On real hardware, reading from an empty slot's ROM space returns whatever data happens to be on the bus. WriteExtendedRom(ushort, byte) Attempts to write to the card's extended ROM space, performing no operation. public void WriteExtendedRom(ushort _, byte _1) Parameters _ ushort The extended ROM offset (ignored). _1 byte The value to write (ignored). Remarks Writes to empty slot extended ROM space are silently ignored, matching real hardware behavior. WriteIO(byte, byte) Attempts to write to the card's I/O space, performing no operation. public void WriteIO(byte _, byte _1) Parameters _ byte The I/O offset (ignored). _1 byte The value to write (ignored). Remarks Writes to empty slots are silently ignored, matching real hardware behavior where writes to unpopulated slots have no effect. WriteRom(byte, byte) Attempts to write to the card's ROM space, performing no operation. public void WriteRom(byte _, byte _1) Parameters _ byte The ROM offset (ignored). _1 byte The value to write (ignored). Remarks Writes to empty slot ROM space are silently ignored, matching real hardware behavior. See Also ICard IConfigurable ISlots ICardFactory

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.RenderContext.html.title: Class RenderContext | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.RenderContext.html.summary: Class RenderContext Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Context for rendering a single video frame, containing frame buffer, memory access, and system status. public class RenderContext Inheritance object RenderContext Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks One-Shot Lifetime: A RenderContext is valid only until it is committed via RenderFrame(RenderContext). After commit, the context is automatically invalidated and attempting to use it will throw InvalidOperationException. Usage Pattern: var context = frameGenerator.AllocateRenderContext(); // Valid frameGenerator.RenderFrame(context); // Commits and invalidates // context is now invalid - do not reuse! Reference Type: RenderContext is a class (reference type), so invalidation state is naturally shared across all references to the same instance. Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. A context should be used by a single thread from allocation through commit. Constructors RenderContext(BitmapDataArray, IDirectMemoryPoolReader, ISystemStatusProvider) Context for rendering a single video frame, containing frame buffer, memory access, and system status. public RenderContext(BitmapDataArray frameBuffer, IDirectMemoryPoolReader memory, ISystemStatusProvider status) Parameters frameBuffer BitmapDataArray The frame buffer where pixels will be written during rendering. memory IDirectMemoryPoolReader Direct memory access for reading video RAM (main and auxiliary banks). status ISystemStatusProvider System status provider for reading soft switch states. Remarks One-Shot Lifetime: A RenderContext is valid only until it is committed via RenderFrame(RenderContext). After commit, the context is automatically invalidated and attempting to use it will throw InvalidOperationException. Usage Pattern: var context = frameGenerator.AllocateRenderContext(); // Valid frameGenerator.RenderFrame(context); // Commits and invalidates // context is now invalid - do not reuse! Reference Type: RenderContext is a class (reference type), so invalidation state is naturally shared across all references to the same instance. Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. A context should be used by a single thread from allocation through commit. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if any parameter is null. All dependencies are required. IsPage2 Gets whether page 2 is active (affects which video memory page is displayed). public bool IsPage2 { get; } Property Value bool Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessing after the context has been invalidated (committed). IsTextMode Gets whether the current video mode is text mode. public bool IsTextMode { get; } Property Value bool Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessing after the context has been invalidated (committed). Memory Direct memory access for reading video RAM (main and auxiliary banks). public IDirectMemoryPoolReader Memory { get; } Property Value IDirectMemoryPoolReader Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessing memory after the context has been invalidated (committed). SystemStatus System status provider for reading soft switch states (video mode, page selection, etc.). public ISystemStatusProvider SystemStatus { get; } Property Value ISystemStatusProvider Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessing system status after the context has been invalidated (committed).
