api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.CharacterR.title: Class CharacterRomProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.CharacterR.summary: Class CharacterRomProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Loads Apple IIe enhanced character ROM from embedded resources and provides glyph access. public class CharacterRomProvider : ICharacterRomProvider Inheritance object CharacterRomProvider Implements ICharacterRomProvider Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Resource: Loads "a2e_enh_video.rom" (2048 bytes) from assembly manifest resources at construction. The character ROM contains 256 character definitions, each 8 bytes tall. Performance: Character ROM is cached in memory for fast O(1) glyph access. All GetGlyph() calls return zero-copy views into the cached ROM buffer. Thread Safety: Thread-safe for reads after construction. The ROM buffer is immutable and never modified after initialization. Constructors CharacterRomProvider() Initializes a new instance and loads the Apple IIe enhanced character ROM. public CharacterRomProvider() Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the embedded ROM resource "a2e_enh_video.rom" cannot be found in the assembly. This typically indicates a build configuration issue where the ROM file was not embedded. Methods GetGlyph(byte, bool, bool) Gets the character ROM data for a specific character. public ReadOnlySpan<byte> GetGlyph(byte ch, bool flashOn, bool altChar) Parameters ch byte The character code (0-255) to retrieve. This is the Apple II screen code (not ASCII), which determines the glyph appearance based on inverse, flashing, and alternate character set attributes. flashOn bool True if flash mode (inverse video) is currently active. When true, flashing characters (codes 0x40-0x7F) are displayed in their inverse state. Flash typically alternates at approximately 2 Hz. altChar bool True if the alternate character set (MouseText) should be used. MouseText provides graphical symbols for user interfaces, replacing control characters in the range 0x40-0x5F when enabled via the SETALTCHAR soft switch. Returns ReadOnlySpan<byte> A read-only span of 8 bytes representing the character glyph, where each byte represents one horizontal scanline from top to bottom. Bits 0-6 of each byte represent pixels (1=lit, 0=dark), with bit 6 being the leftmost pixel. Bit 7 is ignored. Remarks The character ROM layout is organized by display attributes, not ASCII values: 0x00-0x1F: Control characters (displayed as inverse uppercase when altChar is false) 0x20-0x3F: Inverse special characters and numbers 0x40-0x5F: Flashing uppercase letters (or MouseText when altChar is true) 0x60-0x7F: Flashing special characters 0x80-0x9F: Control characters displayed as normal uppercase 0xA0-0xBF: Normal special characters and numbers 0xC0-0xDF: Normal uppercase letters 0xE0-0xFF: Normal lowercase letters and special characters

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.EmulatorSt.title: Class EmulatorStateProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.EmulatorSt.summary: Class EmulatorStateProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Reactive provider for emulator execution state, using a BehaviorSubject to publish state changes. public sealed class EmulatorStateProvider : IEmulatorState Inheritance object EmulatorStateProvider Implements IEmulatorState Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Implementation: Uses System.Reactive.Subjects.BehaviorSubject<T> to maintain the current state and broadcast updates to all subscribers. The BehaviorSubject guarantees that new subscribers immediately receive the most recent state. Thread Safety: BehaviorSubject is thread-safe for publishing and subscribing. Multiple threads can safely call Update(StateSnapshot) and subscribe to Stream. Initial State: Initialized with a default snapshot (PC=0, SP=0, Cycles=0, not running). Control Methods: RequestPause(), RequestContinue(), and RequestStep() are currently placeholders. These will be implemented when the execution control loop is integrated with this provider. Properties Stream Observable stream of emulator state snapshots. public IObservable<StateSnapshot> Stream { get; } Property Value IObservable<StateSnapshot> Remarks Uses a System.Reactive.Subjects.BehaviorSubject<T> which guarantees that new subscribers immediately receive the most recent state snapshot, then receive all subsequent updates. Methods GetCurrent() Gets the current emulator state snapshot. public StateSnapshot GetCurrent() Returns StateSnapshot Remarks Returns the current value of the BehaviorSubject (most recently published snapshot). This is a synchronous operation that does not involve subscribing to the stream. RequestContinue() Requests the emulator to continue execution. public void RequestContinue() Remarks TODO: This is a placeholder. Implementation will signal the emulator's execution controller to resume execution from the paused state. RequestPause() Requests the emulator to pause execution. public void RequestPause() Remarks TODO: This is a placeholder. Implementation will signal the emulator's execution controller (likely via CancellationToken or state flag) to pause execution at the next safe point (typically after completing the current instruction). RequestStep() Requests the emulator to execute a single instruction step. public void RequestStep() Remarks TODO: This is a placeholder. Implementation will signal the emulator to execute exactly one instruction, then pause again. Used for debugging and single-stepping through code. Update(StateSnapshot) Updates the emulator state with a new snapshot. public void Update(StateSnapshot snapshot) Parameters snapshot StateSnapshot Remarks Publishes the new snapshot to all active subscribers via Stream. This method is typically called by the emulator's main execution loop after each instruction or at regular intervals.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.FrameGener.title: Class FrameGenerator | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.FrameGener.summary: Class FrameGenerator Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Generates Apple II video frames by coordinating bitmap rendering, memory access, and system status. Produces annotated frames for downstream consumers (e.g., NTSC post-processing, GUI display). public class FrameGenerator : IFrameGenerator Inheritance object FrameGenerator Implements IFrameGenerator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Architecture: This implementation acts as a facade/coordinator, delegating the actual pixel rendering work to an IDisplayBitmapRenderer while managing the lifecycle and annotation of frame buffers. Frame Annotation: After rendering, this class annotates the frame with display mode metadata (IsGraphics, IsMixed) by setting properties on IFrameProvider. This allows downstream consumers (NTSC renderers, display adapters) to access mode information without requiring their own ISystemStatusProvider reference. Thread Safety: Not inherently thread-safe. Frame generation should be called from a single thread (typically the emulator thread or a dedicated rendering thread). The underlying IFrameProvider handles thread-safe buffer swapping between renderer and consumer. Constructors FrameGenerator(IFrameProvider, IDirectMemoryPoolReader, ISystemStatusProvider, IDisplayBitmapRenderer) Initializes a new instance of the FrameGenerator class. public FrameGenerator(IFrameProvider frameProvider, IDirectMemoryPoolReader memReader, ISystemStatusProvider statusProvider, IDisplayBitmapRenderer renderer) Parameters frameProvider IFrameProvider Provider for double-buffered frame storage. Supplies writable frame buffers and manages buffer swapping after rendering completes. memReader IDirectMemoryPoolReader Direct memory access for reading video RAM (main and auxiliary banks). Used by the renderer to fetch character data, graphics pixels, etc. statusProvider ISystemStatusProvider System status provider for reading soft switch states (video mode, page selection, character set, etc.). Used to determine rendering behavior. renderer IDisplayBitmapRenderer The bitmap renderer implementation that converts video memory into displayable pixels. Typically LegacyBitmapRenderer or a future optimized/NTSC renderer. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if any parameter is null. All dependencies are required for frame generation. Methods AllocateRenderContext() Allocates and initializes a render context for frame generation. public RenderContext AllocateRenderContext() Returns RenderContext A RenderContext containing the frame buffer, memory access, and system status required for rendering a frame. Remarks This method creates a render context that packages together all the dependencies needed for frame rendering: Frame buffer - The writable bitmap where pixels will be rendered Memory access - Direct access to video memory (main and auxiliary) System status - Current soft switch states (video mode, page selection, etc.) The allocated context is typically short-lived, used for rendering a single frame and then discarded. For performance, the underlying frame buffer may be reused through a double-buffering mechanism managed by the IFrameProvider. This method should be called once per frame, typically in response to a VBlank event or refresh timer tick (~60 Hz). RenderFrame(RenderContext) Renders the current Apple IIe video state into the provided context's frame buffer. public void RenderFrame(RenderContext context) Parameters context RenderContext The RenderContext containing the frame buffer, memory access, and system status. Typically obtained from AllocateRenderContext(). As a reference type, the context's invalidation state is shared across all references. Remarks This method invokes the configured renderer (typically IDisplayBitmapRenderer) to convert the current video memory contents and mode settings into a displayable bitmap. The rendering process: Examines the system status to determine active video mode (text/graphics, mixed, etc.) Reads video memory from the appropriate pages (main/auxiliary, page 1/2) Applies character ROM lookups for text modes or color generation for graphics modes Writes the resulting pixels to the context's frame buffer Invalidates the context to prevent reuse (implementation detail) This method should complete quickly (ideally under 16ms for 60 fps) to maintain smooth frame rates. The actual rendering work is delegated to the renderer implementation, which may employ various optimizations (dirty rectangle tracking, scanline caching, etc.). Context Lifetime: After this method completes, the provided context is invalidated and cannot be reused. Attempting to access properties or call methods on the context after this method returns will throw InvalidOperationException. Allocate a new context for each frame via AllocateRenderContext().

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.FrameProvi.title: Class FrameProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.FrameProvi.summary: Class FrameProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Provides double-buffered frame management for Apple II display with dimension validation. public sealed class FrameProvider : IFrameProvider Inheritance object FrameProvider Implements IFrameProvider Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Implementation: This class manages two BitmapDataArray instances as front and back buffers. Frame dimensions are derived from Width and Height static properties and validated at construction. Buffer Swap: Uses C# tuple swap pattern (_back, _front) = (_front, _back) for atomic buffer exchange. This ensures thread-safe swapping without intermediate variables. Validation: Constructor performs strict validation to ensure: Front and back buffers have matching dimensions Dimensions match Apple IIe standard (560×192 pixels) These checks catch configuration errors early and prevent rendering corruption. Thread Safety: The buffer swap in CommitWritable() is atomic, but the caller must ensure only one thread calls rendering methods. The front buffer can be safely read by the UI thread while the back buffer is being written by the rendering thread. Display Mode Metadata: IsGraphics and IsMixed properties are set by FrameGenerator after rendering to provide hints to downstream consumers (NTSC color generation, display adapters). These are not thread-safe and should only be set by the rendering thread. Constructors FrameProvider() Initializes a new instance of the FrameProvider class with dimension validation. public FrameProvider() Remarks Initialization Sequence: Allocates front and back BitmapDataArray buffers Queries buffer dimensions from static properties Validates front and back buffers have matching dimensions Validates dimensions match Apple IIe standard (560×192) Sets Width and Height properties Why Validate? Although the dimensions are determined by BitmapDataArray constants, explicit validation: Catches bugs if BitmapDataArray is refactored incorrectly Documents the expected dimensions (560×192) for Apple IIe Provides clear error messages if configuration is wrong Performance: All validation happens once at construction. Runtime frame operations have no validation overhead. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if front and back buffer dimensions don't match, or if dimensions don't match Apple IIe standard (560×192). These exceptions indicate a configuration error in BitmapDataArray and should never occur in production with correct constants. Properties Height Gets the height of the frame in scanlines. public int Height { get; } Property Value int The frame height in scanlines. For Apple IIe, this is 192 scanlines (24 text rows × 8 scanlines per row). IsGraphics Gets or sets whether the emulator is in graphics mode (as opposed to text mode). public bool IsGraphics { get; set; } Property Value bool True if displaying graphics (lo-res or hi-res); false if displaying text mode. Remarks This property reflects the state of the TEXT soft switch. When false (text mode), the display shows 40-column or 80-column text. When true (graphics mode), the display shows lo-res (40×48 color blocks) or hi-res (280×192 pixels) graphics. The primary purpose of this flag is to give the UI renderer a hint to use NTSC color generation and fringing when applicable. IsMixed Gets or sets whether the emulator is in mixed text/graphics mode. public bool IsMixed { get; set; } Property Value bool True if displaying mixed mode (graphics on top 20 rows, text on bottom 4 rows); false if displaying pure text or pure graphics. Remarks This property reflects the state of the MIXED soft switch. When true, the top 160 scanlines display graphics content and the bottom 32 scanlines (4 text rows) display text. This mode is commonly used in Apple II games to show graphics with a text status line at the bottom. The primary purpose of this flag is to give the UI renderer a hint that the bottom 4 lines of the display are text and can optionally be excluded from NTSC color generation to reduce fringing of the text. Width Gets the width of the frame in pixels. public int Width { get; } Property Value int The frame width in pixels. For Apple IIe standard modes, this is typically 560 pixels (280 pixels doubled for HGR, individual for DHGR, 14 (or 7) pixels per character × 40 (or 80) columns). Remarks Currently, this value is determined by the default implementation of BitmapDataArray. In a future refactoring, the frame provider may dictate dimensions at creation time to support additional graphics modes. The pixel width represents the final rendered output width, which may include horizontal doubling to achieve proper aspect ratio on square-pixel displays. Methods BorrowWritable() Gets the back buffer for writing and composing the next frame. public BitmapDataArray BorrowWritable() Returns BitmapDataArray A BitmapDataArray that can be written to for composing the next frame. This buffer is not visible to the display until CommitWritable() is called. Remarks This method is typically called by the rendering subsystem at the start of each frame generation cycle. The caller should render the complete frame into this buffer, then call CommitWritable() to make it visible. The term "Borrow" indicates that the caller has temporary exclusive access to this buffer for rendering purposes. CommitWritable() Swaps the front and back buffers and raises the FrameAvailable event. public void CommitWritable() Remarks This method atomically swaps the front and back buffers, making the newly rendered frame visible while providing the previous front buffer as the new back buffer for the next frame. After the swap, the FrameAvailable event is raised to notify the UI layer that a new frame is ready for display. This method should be called by the rendering subsystem after completing a frame (typically once per VBlank period, approximately 60 times per second for Apple IIe). GetFrame() Gets the current front buffer for reading and displaying. public BitmapDataArray GetFrame() Returns BitmapDataArray A BitmapDataArray containing the most recently committed frame, ready for display. This buffer is stable and will not change until the next call to CommitWritable(). Remarks This method is typically called by the UI layer in response to the FrameAvailable event to retrieve the completed frame for display. The returned buffer should be treated as read-only to prevent race conditions with the rendering thread. Events FrameAvailable Raised after a new frame has been committed and is available for display. public event EventHandler? FrameAvailable Event Type EventHandler Remarks This event is fired by CommitWritable() after the front and back buffers have been swapped. Subscribers (typically the UI layer) should respond by reading the front buffer via GetFrame() and updating the display. This event is typically raised at the video refresh rate (approximately 60 Hz for Apple IIe).

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.LegacyBitm.title: Class LegacyBitmapRenderer | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.LegacyBitm.summary: Class LegacyBitmapRenderer Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Basic monochrome bitmap renderer for Apple IIe video modes (stopgap implementation). public class LegacyBitmapRenderer : IDisplayBitmapRenderer Inheritance object LegacyBitmapRenderer Implements IDisplayBitmapRenderer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks ⚠️ TEMPORARY IMPLEMENTATION: This renderer is a stopgap solution providing basic Apple IIe monochrome bitmap output. It will be replaced by a more accurate scanline-based renderer in a future update. Monochrome Output: This renderer (and its replacement) generates MONOCHROME BITMAPS ONLY. NTSC color artifact generation is handled by downstream UI renderers, not by this class. The output is a binary (on/off) bitmap representing what pixels would be lit on a monochrome monitor. Supported Modes: Text Mode: 40-column (fully supported), 80-column (basic support) Lo-Res Graphics: 40×48 blocks (fully supported) Hi-Res Graphics: 280×192 pixels (basic support) Mixed Mode: Graphics + 4-line text status area (supported) Known Limitations: Cell-based rendering instead of scanline-by-scanline Simplified pixel fringing logic Double hi-res (DHGR) partially implemented No accurate timing simulation Rendering Approach: This renderer uses a cell-based approach, iterating through 24 rows × 40 columns and rendering each 8-scanline cell based on the current video mode. Memory addresses are calculated using Apple IIe's interleaved scanline pattern, and pixels are written to bitplane 0 of the frame buffer. Constructors LegacyBitmapRenderer(ICharacterRomProvider) Initializes a new instance of the LegacyBitmapRenderer class. public LegacyBitmapRenderer(ICharacterRomProvider charRomProvider) Parameters charRomProvider ICharacterRomProvider Character ROM provider for text mode rendering. Provides 8-row glyph data for each character, with support for flashing characters, MouseText, and alternate character set. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if charRomProvider is null. Methods Render(RenderContext) Renders the current Apple IIe video state into the provided frame buffer. public void Render(RenderContext context) Parameters context RenderContext A RenderContext containing the frame buffer to render into, direct memory access for reading video RAM, and system status information including video mode soft switches. Remarks This method examines the video mode flags in the context (text/graphics, mixed mode, hi-res/lo-res, page selection) and renders the appropriate video content: Reads from the correct video memory page (page 1 or page 2) based on soft switches Interprets memory contents according to the active video mode Handles character ROM lookups for text mode, including flashing characters and MouseText Applies Apple IIe color generation rules for graphics modes Renders mixed mode by combining graphics and text regions Outputs pixels to the frame buffer in a format ready for display This method should be called once per video frame (typically 60 times per second) to keep the display synchronized with video memory changes. The rendering process directly reads memory through IDirectMemoryPoolReader to access both main and auxiliary video pages without going through the CPU bus.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.NullFloati.title: Class NullFloatingBusProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.NullFloati.summary: Class NullFloatingBusProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Simple floating bus provider that always returns zero. public sealed class NullFloatingBusProvider : IFloatingBusProvider Inheritance object NullFloatingBusProvider Implements IFloatingBusProvider Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides a simple, deterministic floating bus implementation for testing, debugging, or scenarios where floating bus accuracy is not required. Behavior: Always returns 0x00 for unmapped reads, regardless of CPU activity or video state. This is the simplest possible floating bus implementation. Use Cases: Unit testing - Predictable, deterministic behavior Initial development - Simplest implementation to get started Debugging - Eliminates floating bus complexity from troubleshooting Performance baseline - Minimal overhead for benchmarking Limitations: This implementation is not accurate to Apple II hardware. Software that relies on floating bus behavior (copy protection, timing loops, video synchronization) will not work correctly. For production use, consider LastValueFloatingBus or CycleAccurateFloatingBus. Performance: Fastest possible implementation - no state, no logic, just returns a constant. JIT compiler may inline this to a single instruction. Methods Read() Reads the floating bus value (always returns 0). public byte Read() Returns byte Always returns 0x00. Remarks This implementation ignores all bus activity and always returns zero. This is not accurate to Apple II hardware behavior, where the floating bus would typically return the last value on the data bus.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.RandomFloa.title: Class RandomFloatingBusProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.RandomFloa.summary: Class RandomFloatingBusProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Floating bus provider that returns random values for each read. public sealed class RandomFloatingBusProvider : IFloatingBusProvider Inheritance object RandomFloatingBusProvider Implements IFloatingBusProvider Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides a randomized floating bus implementation that simulates unpredictable bus noise, useful for testing software robustness and detecting code that incorrectly relies on specific floating bus values. Behavior: Returns a random byte value (0x00-0xFF) for each read operation, simulating the unpredictable nature of reading from unmapped memory addresses where the data bus may contain arbitrary values from previous bus activity. Use Cases: Testing - Verifies software doesn't depend on specific floating bus values Debugging - Helps identify uninitialized memory reads or bus conflicts Robustness validation - Ensures code handles arbitrary floating bus data Copy protection analysis - Some protection schemes test for random vs. deterministic behavior Limitations: This implementation is not accurate to Apple II hardware behavior. Real hardware floating bus values are not truly random - they reflect the last value on the data bus (often video data during screen refresh). Software that relies on specific floating bus patterns (video synchronization, timing loops, some copy protection) will not work correctly. For production use, consider LastValueFloatingBus or CycleAccurateFloatingBus. Randomness: Uses Random with a time-based seed for non-cryptographic randomness. Each instance maintains its own random number generator to ensure deterministic behavior within a single emulator session if needed. Performance: Slightly slower than NullFloatingBusProvider due to random number generation overhead, but still negligible compared to other emulation operations. The random number generator is instance-scoped to avoid thread-safety issues. Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Each instance should be used by a single thread (typically the emulator worker thread). The internal Random instance is not synchronized for performance reasons. Constructors RandomFloatingBusProvider() Initializes a new instance of the RandomFloatingBusProvider class. public RandomFloatingBusProvider() Remarks Creates a new random number generator with a time-based seed. Each instance will produce a different sequence of values, making each emulator session unique. RandomFloatingBusProvider(int) Initializes a new instance of the RandomFloatingBusProvider class with a specific seed. public RandomFloatingBusProvider(int seed) Parameters seed int Seed value for the random number generator. Remarks Using a specific seed allows for reproducible behavior across multiple runs, which is useful for: Debugging - Reproduce specific floating bus sequences that trigger bugs Testing - Ensure consistent test results across runs Save states - Maintain consistent floating bus behavior when loading saved state Example: // Create deterministic floating bus provider for testing var floatingBus = new RandomFloatingBusProvider(12345); // Same seed produces same sequence var floatingBus2 = new RandomFloatingBusProvider(12345); Assert.Equal(floatingBus.Read(), floatingBus2.Read()); Methods Read() Reads the floating bus value (returns a random byte). public byte Read() Returns byte A random byte value between 0x00 and 0xFF. Remarks Each call returns a new random value. This simulates the unpredictable nature of reading from unmapped memory where the data bus contains arbitrary values. Distribution: Uses uniform distribution across the full byte range (0-255). All values have equal probability. Performance: Generates a random integer and masks to byte range. Typical execution time is ~10-20ns on modern processors.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.StateSnaps.title: Class StateSnapshot | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.StateSnaps.summary: Class StateSnapshot Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Immutable snapshot of the emulator's execution state at a specific point in time. public record StateSnapshot : IEquatable<StateSnapshot> Inheritance object StateSnapshot Implements IEquatable<StateSnapshot> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This record is immutable and thread-safe. Each state change produces a new instance, which is published through Stream for reactive subscribers. Constructors StateSnapshot(ushort, byte, ulong, int?, bool, bool) Immutable snapshot of the emulator's execution state at a specific point in time. public StateSnapshot(ushort PC, byte SP, ulong Cycles, int? LineNumber, bool IsRunning, bool IsPaused) Parameters PC ushort Program Counter (16-bit address of the next instruction to execute) SP byte Stack Pointer (8-bit offset into the 6502 stack page at 0x0100-0x01FF) Cycles ulong Total CPU cycles executed since emulator start or reset LineNumber int? Optional line number in disassembly/source code corresponding to the current PC. Null if no source mapping is available. IsRunning bool True if the emulator is actively executing instructions (RunAsync is active). False if stopped or not yet started. IsPaused bool True if the emulator is paused (execution suspended but can be resumed). False if running or stopped. Remarks This record is immutable and thread-safe. Each state change produces a new instance, which is published through Stream for reactive subscribers. Properties Cycles Total CPU cycles executed since emulator start or reset public ulong Cycles { get; init; } Property Value ulong IsPaused True if the emulator is paused (execution suspended but can be resumed). False if running or stopped. public bool IsPaused { get; init; } Property Value bool IsRunning True if the emulator is actively executing instructions (RunAsync is active). False if stopped or not yet started. public bool IsRunning { get; init; } Property Value bool LineNumber Optional line number in disassembly/source code corresponding to the current PC. Null if no source mapping is available. public int? LineNumber { get; init; } Property Value int? PC Program Counter (16-bit address of the next instruction to execute) public ushort PC { get; init; } Property Value ushort SP Stack Pointer (8-bit offset into the 6502 stack page at 0x0100-0x01FF) public byte SP { get; init; } Property Value byte

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.SystemRomP.title: Class SystemRomProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.SystemRomP.summary: Class SystemRomProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Provides Apple IIe system ROM storage and access. public sealed class SystemRomProvider : ISystemRomProvider, IMemory Inheritance object SystemRomProvider Implements ISystemRomProvider IMemory Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Manages the 16KB system ROM ($C000-$FFFF) including I/O firmware, peripheral ROMs, Monitor ROM, and Applesoft BASIC. The ROM can be loaded from either a file or an embedded resource. ROM Layout (16KB): $C000-$C0FF (256 bytes) - I/O space firmware $C100-$C7FF (1792 bytes) - Internal peripheral ROM (7 x 256 bytes) $C800-$CFFF (2KB) - Extended internal ROM $D000-$DFFF (4KB) - Monitor ROM / Language Card bank switching area $E000-$FFFF (8KB) - Applesoft BASIC + reset vector / Language Card common area Loading Sources: File System: Standard file path (e.g., "roms/AppleIIe.rom") Embedded Resource: Use "res:" prefix (e.g., "res:Pandowdy.EmuCore.Resources.a2e_enh_c-f.rom") Address Mapping: The ROM provider's address space starts at offset 0, representing $C000. To access $D000, use offset 0x1000. To access $FFFF, use offset 0x3FFF. Constructors SystemRomProvider(string) Initializes a new instance of the SystemRomProvider class and loads ROM. public SystemRomProvider(string filename) Parameters filename string Path to the ROM file or resource identifier. Must be exactly 16KB (16,384 bytes). Use "res:" prefix for embedded resources (e.g., "res:Pandowdy.EmuCore.Resources.a2e_enh_c-f.rom"). Remarks File System Example: var rom = new SystemRomProvider("roms/AppleIIe.rom"); Embedded Resource Example: var rom = new SystemRomProvider("res:Pandowdy.EmuCore.Resources.a2e_enh_c-f.rom"); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if filename is null. FileNotFoundException Thrown if the ROM file/resource cannot be found. InvalidDataException Thrown if the ROM is not exactly 16KB or cannot be read. Properties this[ushort] Gets or sets a byte at the specified address. Setter is a no-op (ROM is read-only). public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; } Parameters address ushort Address within the ROM (0x0000-0x3FFF). Property Value byte Byte value at the specified address. Size Gets the size of the ROM in bytes (always 16KB). public int Size { get; } Property Value int Methods Read(ushort) Reads a byte from the ROM at the specified address. public byte Read(ushort address) Parameters address ushort Address within the ROM (0x0000-0x3FFF representing $C000-$FFFF). Returns byte Byte value at the specified address. Remarks Address Mapping: 0x0000 = $C000 (I/O firmware start) 0x1000 = $D000 (Monitor ROM start / Language Card bank area) 0x2000 = $E000 (BASIC ROM start / Language Card common area) 0x3FFF = $FFFF (End of ROM / Reset vector high byte) Exceptions IndexOutOfRangeException Thrown if address is beyond ROM size. Write(ushort, byte) Write operation - ROM is read-only, this is a no-op. public void Write(ushort address, byte data) Parameters address ushort Address (ignored). data byte Data (ignored). Remarks ROM is read-only. Writes are silently ignored to maintain IMemory compatibility. In real hardware, ROM writes have no effect.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.SystemStat.title: Class SystemStatusProvider | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.SystemStat.summary: Class SystemStatusProvider Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Provides observable system status tracking with soft switch response integration. public sealed class SystemStatusProvider : ISystemStatusProvider, ISoftSwitchResponder Inheritance object SystemStatusProvider Implements ISystemStatusProvider ISoftSwitchResponder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Dual Role: This class serves two roles in the emulator architecture: ISystemStatusProvider - Read-only observable access for consumers (renderers, debuggers, UI) to query and subscribe to system state changes. ISoftSwitchResponder - Write interface for the bus/soft switch manager to update state when soft switches are toggled by programs. Observable Pattern: Uses System.Reactive.Subjects.BehaviorSubject<T> to provide reactive updates via Stream. The BehaviorSubject replays the current state to new subscribers, ensuring they always have the latest snapshot. Traditional .NET events are also supported via Changed. Default State: Initializes to Apple IIe power-on defaults: Text mode enabled Internal ROM enabled (INTCXROM = true) All other switches off This matches the behavior of a freshly powered-on Apple IIe before the monitor runs. Thread Safety: Updates via Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>) are not inherently thread-safe. The caller (typically VA2MBus) must ensure serialized access from a single thread. Reads are safe from any thread due to immutable snapshots. Change Notification Order: When state changes, the observable stream (Stream) is notified first, followed by the Changed event. This ensures reactive subscribers see updates before event handlers. Constructors SystemStatusProvider() Initializes a new instance of the SystemStatusProvider class. public SystemStatusProvider() Remarks Creates the reactive subject with the default power-on state, ensuring new subscribers immediately receive the current system status. Properties Current Gets the current system status as an immutable snapshot. public SystemStatusSnapshot Current { get; } Property Value SystemStatusSnapshot A SystemStatusSnapshot containing all current soft switch states and system flags. Remarks This property provides a point-in-time snapshot of the entire system state, useful for comparing states, logging, or passing to components that need multiple status values atomically. State80Store Gets the state of the 80STORE soft switch ($C000/$C001). public bool State80Store { get; } Property Value bool True if 80STORE is enabled (page 2 redirected to auxiliary memory); false otherwise. Remarks When enabled, page 2 video memory is redirected to auxiliary RAM, enabling 80-column text mode and double hi-res graphics with independent video pages. StateAltCharSet Gets the state of the ALTCHAR soft switch ($C00E/$C00F). public bool StateAltCharSet { get; } Property Value bool True if using MouseText character set; false if using standard. Remarks Selects between the standard Apple II character set and the alternate MouseText character set. MouseText provides graphical symbols for building text-based UIs. StateAltZp Gets the state of the ALTZP soft switch ($C008/$C009). public bool StateAltZp { get; } Property Value bool True if using auxiliary zero page/stack; false if using main. Remarks Controls whether zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) access auxiliary memory. Allows programs to maintain separate CPU contexts. StateAnn0 Gets the state of annunciator 0 ($C058/$C059). public bool StateAnn0 { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 0 is on; false if off. Remarks General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. Some games use annunciators for audio effects or peripheral control. StateAnn1 Gets the state of annunciator 1 ($C05A/$C05B). public bool StateAnn1 { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 1 is on; false if off. Remarks General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. StateAnn2 Gets the state of annunciator 2 ($C05C/$C05D). public bool StateAnn2 { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 2 is on; false if off. Remarks General-purpose output signal, rarely used in standard software. StateAnn3_DGR Gets the state of annunciator 3 / double hi-res enable ($C05E/$C05F). public bool StateAnn3_DGR { get; } Property Value bool True if annunciator 3 is on; false if off. Remarks Annunciator 3 doubles as the double hi-res (DHR) enable switch. Important: The logic is inverted for DHGR - when this value is false (annunciator off), double hi-res mode is enabled. When this value is true (annunciator on), double hi-res is disabled. StateFlashOn Gets the current flash state for flashing characters. public bool StateFlashOn { get; } Property Value bool True if flash characters should be displayed in inverse; false for normal. Remarks The Apple IIe automatically alternates the display of flashing characters (codes $40-$7F) between normal and inverse video at approximately 2 Hz. This property reflects the current phase of that cycle. StateHiRes Gets the state of the HIRES soft switch ($C056/$C057). public bool StateHiRes { get; } Property Value bool True if hi-res graphics mode; false if lo-res graphics mode. Remarks In graphics mode (TEXT off), controls whether to display hi-res graphics (280×192 pixels, 6 colors) or lo-res graphics (40×48 color blocks, 16 colors). Has no effect when TEXT mode is enabled. StateHighRead Gets the language card read enable state. public bool StateHighRead { get; } Property Value bool True if reading from language card RAM; false if reading from ROM. Remarks Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF access the language card RAM or the built-in ROM. Independent of write enable, allowing read-from-ROM, write-to-RAM configurations. StateHighWrite Gets the language card write enable state. public bool StateHighWrite { get; } Property Value bool True if writing to language card RAM is enabled; false if write-protected. Remarks Controls whether writes to the $D000-$FFFF range modify the language card RAM. Write protection is engaged through a two-access sequence to prevent accidental modification of critical code. StateIntCxRom Gets the state of the INTCXROM soft switch ($C006/$C007). public bool StateIntCxRom { get; } Property Value bool True if using internal ROM; false if using slot ROMs. Remarks Controls whether the $C100-$CFFF range accesses internal ROM or peripheral card slot ROMs. Typically set by the monitor during initialization. StateMixed Gets the state of the MIXED soft switch ($C052/$C053). public bool StateMixed { get; } Property Value bool True if mixed mode enabled; false if full-screen mode. Remarks When true and in graphics mode, displays graphics on the top 20 rows (160 scanlines) and text on the bottom 4 rows (32 scanlines). Commonly used in games to show graphics with a text status line. Has no effect in text mode. StatePage2 Gets the state of the PAGE2 soft switch ($C054/$C055). public bool StatePage2 { get; } Property Value bool True if displaying page 2; false if displaying page 1. Remarks Selects which video page is displayed. Page 1 uses $0400-$07FF (text) or $2000-$3FFF (hi-res). Page 2 uses $0800-$0BFF (text) or $4000-$5FFF (hi-res). Can be used for page flipping animation. StatePb0 Gets the state of pushbutton 0 ($C061). public bool StatePb0 { get; } Property Value bool True if button 0 is pressed; false if released. Remarks Represents the state of game controller button 0. The high bit of the byte read from $C061 indicates the button state. StatePb1 Gets the state of pushbutton 1 ($C062). public bool StatePb1 { get; } Property Value bool True if button 1 is pressed; false if released. Remarks Represents the state of game controller button 1. The high bit of the byte read from $C062 indicates the button state. StatePb2 Gets the state of pushbutton 2 ($C063). public bool StatePb2 { get; } Property Value bool True if button 2 is pressed; false if released. Remarks Represents the state of game controller button 2. The high bit of the byte read from $C063 indicates the button state. StatePreWrite Gets the language card pre-write state. public bool StatePreWrite { get; } Property Value bool True if the pre-write sequence is active; false otherwise. Remarks The language card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing. The first access sets pre-write mode. A second consecutive access to the same address enables write mode. This is an internal state for the write protection mechanism. StateRamRd Gets the state of the RAMRD soft switch ($C002/$C003). public bool StateRamRd { get; } Property Value bool True if reading from auxiliary memory; false if reading from main memory. Remarks Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is used for memory reads. Allows programs to access the Apple IIe's extended 128KB memory space. StateRamWrt Gets the state of the RAMWRT soft switch ($C004/$C005). public bool StateRamWrt { get; } Property Value bool True if writing to auxiliary memory; false if writing to main memory. Remarks Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is used for memory writes. Independent from RAMRD, allowing asymmetric read/write configurations. StateShow80Col Gets the state of the 80VID soft switch ($C00C/$C00D). public bool StateShow80Col { get; } Property Value bool True if 80-column mode enabled; false if 40-column mode. Remarks Enables 80-column text mode, which uses both main and auxiliary memory to display 80 characters per line (alternating bytes from main and aux). When false, standard 40-column text mode is used. StateSlotC3Rom Gets the state of the SLOTC3ROM soft switch ($C00A/$C00B). public bool StateSlotC3Rom { get; } Property Value bool True if using slot C3 ROM; false if using internal ROM. Remarks Controls whether the $C300-$C3FF range (slot 3) uses peripheral card ROM or internal ROM. Independent of INTCXROM. Commonly used for 80-column card firmware. StateTextMode Gets the state of the TEXT soft switch ($C050/$C051). public bool StateTextMode { get; } Property Value bool True if text mode; false if graphics mode. Remarks Controls the primary video mode. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays lo-res or hi-res graphics, depending on the HIRES switch. StateUseBank1 Gets the language card bank selection state. public bool StateUseBank1 { get; } Property Value bool True if bank 1 is selected; false if bank 2 is selected. Remarks The language card provides two 4KB RAM banks for the $D000-$DFFF address space. Bank 1 is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 is at $D000-$DFFF. Stream Gets an observable stream of system status snapshots. public IObservable<SystemStatusSnapshot> Stream { get; } Property Value IObservable<SystemStatusSnapshot> An IObservable<T> that emits snapshots whenever system status changes. Remarks This observable stream provides the same notifications as the Changed event, but in a reactive (Rx.NET) format. Useful for composing reactive pipelines, throttling updates, or combining with other observables. Example usage: statusProvider.Stream .Throttle(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(16)) .ObserveOn(RxApp.MainThreadScheduler) .Subscribe(snapshot => UpdateUI(snapshot)); Methods Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>) Internal mutation method for updating the status snapshot. public void Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder> mutator) Parameters mutator Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder> A builder action that modifies the snapshot state Remarks For internal use by emulator core only. This method is called by the bus or soft switch manager to update the system status when switches are toggled. It uses a builder pattern to efficiently update the immutable snapshot and notify subscribers. Consumers should not call this method - use the read-only properties and subscribe to Changed or Stream instead. Set80Store(bool) Called when the 80STORE soft switch is changed ($C000/$C001). public void Set80Store(bool store80) Parameters store80 bool True if 80STORE is enabled; false if disabled Remarks When enabled, page 2 memory ($0800-$0BFF for text, $2000-$3FFF for hi-res) is redirected to auxiliary memory when PAGE2 is set. This is used for 80-column text mode and double hi-res graphics to provide independent main and aux video pages. Set80Vid(bool) Called when the 80VID soft switch is changed ($C00C/$C00D). public void Set80Vid(bool vid) Parameters vid bool True for 80-column mode; false for 40-column mode Remarks Enables 80-column text mode, which uses both main and auxiliary memory to display 80 characters per line (alternating bytes from main and aux memory). When false, standard 40-column text mode is used. SetAltChar(bool) Called when the ALTCHAR soft switch is changed ($C00E/$C00F). public void SetAltChar(bool altChar) Parameters altChar bool True for MouseText character set; false for standard Remarks Selects between the standard Apple II character set and the alternate MouseText character set. MouseText provides graphical symbols for building text-based UIs (boxes, arrows, icons) in the $40-$5F character range. SetAltZp(bool) Called when the ALTZP soft switch is changed ($C008/$C009). public void SetAltZp(bool altZp) Parameters altZp bool True to use auxiliary zero page/stack; false for main Remarks Controls whether zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) access main or auxiliary memory. When true, these critical CPU regions use auxiliary memory, allowing programs to maintain separate zero page and stack contexts. SetAn0(bool) Called when annunciator 0 is changed ($C058/$C059). public void SetAn0(bool an0) Parameters an0 bool True if annunciator 0 is on; false if off Remarks Annunciator 0 is a general-purpose output signal. Originally intended for controlling external devices, rarely used in standard software. Some games use annunciators for audio effects or peripheral control. SetAn1(bool) Called when annunciator 1 is changed ($C05A/$C05B). public void SetAn1(bool an1) Parameters an1 bool True if annunciator 1 is on; false if off Remarks Annunciator 1 is a general-purpose output signal. Originally intended for controlling external devices, rarely used in standard software. SetAn2(bool) Called when annunciator 2 is changed ($C05C/$C05D). public void SetAn2(bool an2) Parameters an2 bool True if annunciator 2 is on; false if off Remarks Annunciator 2 is a general-purpose output signal. Originally intended for controlling external devices, rarely used in standard software. SetAn3(bool) Called when annunciator 3 is changed ($C05E/$C05F). public void SetAn3(bool an3) Parameters an3 bool True if annunciator 3 is on; false if off Remarks Annunciator 3 doubles as the double hi-res (DHR) enable switch. Important: The logic is inverted - when this annunciator is off (false), double hi-res mode is enabled. When the annunciator is on (true), double hi-res is disabled. This backwards behavior is a quirk of the Apple IIe hardware design. Double hi-res mode (560×192 pixels, 16 colors) uses both main and auxiliary video pages. When not used for DHGR control, this functions as a general-purpose output signal like the other annunciators. SetBank1(bool) Called when the language card bank selection changes ($C080-$C08F). public void SetBank1(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool True for bank 1 (D000-DFFF physical at C000); false for bank 2 (at D000) Remarks The language card provides two 4KB RAM banks that can be mapped into the $D000-$DFFF address space. Bank 1 is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 is at $D000-$DFFF. This is often confusing because bank 1's physical location overlaps with I/O space in the address map. SetHiRes(bool) Called when the HIRES soft switch is changed ($C056/$C057). public void SetHiRes(bool hires) Parameters hires bool True for hi-res graphics; false for lo-res graphics Remarks In graphics mode (TEXT off), controls whether to display hi-res graphics (280×192 pixels, 6 colors) or lo-res graphics (40×48 color blocks, 16 colors). Has no effect when TEXT mode is enabled. SetHighRead(bool) Called when the language card read enable state changes ($C080-$C08F). public void SetHighRead(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool True to read from language card RAM; false to read from ROM Remarks Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF access the language card RAM or the built-in ROM. When true, the selected bank ($D000-$DFFF) and upper 8KB ($E000-$FFFF) are read from RAM. When false, ROM is visible instead. Independent of write enable, allowing read-from-ROM, write-to-RAM configurations. SetHighWrite(bool) Called when the language card write enable state changes ($C080-$C08F). public void SetHighWrite(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool True if writing to language card RAM is enabled; false if write-protected Remarks Controls whether writes to the $D000-$FFFF range modify the language card RAM or are ignored. Write protection is engaged through a two-access sequence to prevent accidental modification of critical code. When false, writes to this region have no effect (ROM remains visible during reads). SetIntCxRom(bool) Called when the INTCXROM soft switch is changed ($C006/$C007). public void SetIntCxRom(bool intCxRom) Parameters intCxRom bool True to use internal ROM; false to use slot ROMs Remarks Controls whether the $C100-$CFFF range accesses internal ROM or peripheral card slot ROMs. When true (internal), the built-in ROM is used. When false, individual slot ROMs can be accessed. Typically set by the monitor during initialization. SetMixed(bool) Called when the MIXED soft switch is changed ($C052/$C053). public void SetMixed(bool mixed) Parameters mixed bool True for mixed mode; false for full-screen mode Remarks When true and in graphics mode, displays graphics on the top 20 rows (160 scanlines) and text on the bottom 4 rows (32 scanlines). Commonly used in games to show graphics with a text status line. Has no effect in text mode. SetPage2(bool) Called when the PAGE2 soft switch is changed ($C054/$C055). public void SetPage2(bool page2) Parameters page2 bool True for page 2; false for page 1 Remarks Selects which video page is displayed. Page 1 uses $0400-$07FF (text) or $2000-$3FFF (hi-res). Page 2 uses $0800-$0BFF (text) or $4000-$5FFF (hi-res). Can be used for page flipping animation. Interaction with 80STORE affects whether page 2 uses main or auxiliary memory. SetPreWrite(bool) Called when the language card pre-write state changes ($C080-$C08F). public void SetPreWrite(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool True if pre-write sequence is active; false otherwise Remarks The language card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing, preventing accidental modification. The first access to certain addresses sets pre-write mode. A second consecutive access to the same address enables write mode. Any other memory access clears pre-write. This is an internal state used to implement the write protection mechanism. SetRamRd(bool) Called when the RAMRD soft switch is changed ($C002/$C003). public void SetRamRd(bool ramRd) Parameters ramRd bool True to read from auxiliary memory; false to read from main memory Remarks Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is read from for memory accesses. This affects most memory regions except those controlled by other soft switches (like 80STORE for video pages). Used for accessing the extended 128KB memory space. SetRamWrt(bool) Called when the RAMWRT soft switch is changed ($C004/$C005). public void SetRamWrt(bool ramWrt) Parameters ramWrt bool True to write to auxiliary memory; false to write to main memory Remarks Controls which 64KB bank (main or auxiliary) is written to for memory accesses. Independent from RAMRD, allowing asymmetric configurations (e.g., read from main, write to aux). Used for accessing the extended 128KB memory space. SetSlotC3Rom(bool) Called when the SLOTC3ROM soft switch is changed ($C00A/$C00B). public void SetSlotC3Rom(bool slotC3Rom) Parameters slotC3Rom bool True to use slot C3 ROM; false to use internal ROM Remarks Controls whether the $C300-$C3FF range (slot 3) uses the peripheral card ROM or internal ROM. Independent of INTCXROM, allowing slot 3 to be controlled separately from other slots. Commonly used for 80-column card firmware. SetText(bool) Called when the TEXT soft switch is changed ($C050/$C051). public void SetText(bool text) Parameters text bool True for text mode; false for graphics mode Remarks Controls the primary video mode. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays lo-res (40×48 color blocks) or hi-res (280×192 pixels) graphics, depending on the HIRES switch. Events Changed Raised when any system status changes. public event EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>? Changed Event Type EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot> Remarks This event fires whenever any soft switch or system flag changes state. The event args contain a SystemStatusSnapshot with the new state. Subscribers can use this for event-driven updates to UI or other components. For reactive programming patterns, consider using Stream instead. MemoryMappingChanged Event raised when any soft switch affecting memory mapping changes. public event EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot>? MemoryMappingChanged Event Type EventHandler<SystemStatusSnapshot> Remarks Fires only when RAMRD, RAMWRT, ALTZP, 80STORE, HIRES, PAGE2, INTCXROM, SLOTC3ROM, HIGHWRITE, BANK1, or HIGHREAD change. Provides the full SystemStatusSnapshot for convenience.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.SystemStat.summary: Class SystemStatusSnapshot Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Immutable snapshot of all Apple IIe system status flags and soft switch states. public record SystemStatusSnapshot : IEquatable<SystemStatusSnapshot> Inheritance object SystemStatusSnapshot Implements IEquatable<SystemStatusSnapshot> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Immutability: This record is immutable - once created, its state cannot change. Any modification requires creating a new snapshot via SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder. This ensures thread-safe reads and clear change tracking through the observable pattern. Complete State Capture: Contains all 24 soft switch states for a single point in time, including memory configuration, video modes, and I/O settings. This allows consumers (renderers, debuggers, UI) to work with a consistent view of system state. Apple IIe Context: These flags directly correspond to Apple IIe hardware soft switches and annunciators, providing authentic emulation of the system's state management. See Apple IIe Technical Reference Manual for detailed switch descriptions. api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.SystemStat.summary: Class SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Mutable builder for creating SystemStatusSnapshot instances. public sealed class SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder Inheritance object SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Builder Pattern: Provides a mutable workspace for constructing new snapshots. All fields can be freely modified before calling Build() to produce an immutable SystemStatusSnapshot. This is more efficient than creating snapshots via the record's with syntax when modifying multiple fields. Usage: Typically used internally by Mutate(Action<SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder>) to batch multiple soft switch updates into a single atomic state change. api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.html.title: Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services.html.summary: Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services Classes CharacterRomProvider Loads Apple IIe enhanced character ROM from embedded resources and provides glyph access. EmulatorStateProvider Reactive provider for emulator execution state, using a BehaviorSubject to publish state changes. FrameGenerator Generates Apple II video frames by coordinating bitmap rendering, memory access, and system status. Produces annotated frames for downstream consumers (e.g., NTSC post-processing, GUI display). FrameProvider Provides double-buffered frame management for Apple II display with dimension validation. LegacyBitmapRenderer Basic monochrome bitmap renderer for Apple IIe video modes (stopgap implementation). NullFloatingBusProvider Simple floating bus provider that always returns zero. RandomFloatingBusProvider Floating bus provider that returns random values for each read. StateSnapshot Immutable snapshot of the emulator's execution state at a specific point in time. SystemRomProvider Provides Apple IIe system ROM storage and access. SystemStatusProvider Provides observable system status tracking with soft switch response integration. SystemStatusSnapshot Immutable snapshot of all Apple IIe system status flags and soft switch states. SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder Mutable builder for creating SystemStatusSnapshot instances.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Slots.html.title: Class Slots | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.Slots.html.summary: Class Slots Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Implements the Apple IIe expansion slot system, managing peripheral cards and their I/O and ROM access. public class Slots : ISlots, IMemory, IConfigurable Inheritance object Slots Implements ISlots IMemory IConfigurable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The Slots class is the concrete implementation of ISlots, providing the complete address decoding logic for the Apple IIe's $C000-$CFFF peripheral I/O range. It coordinates between peripheral cards, system ROM, and soft switch settings to determine which device responds to memory accesses. Architecture: The slots system maintains an array of 8 card positions (0-7), where slot 0 is reserved for system use and slots 1-7 are available for peripheral cards. Empty slots are filled with NullCard instances that return null for all operations, causing floating bus behavior. Address Decoding Priority: INTCXROM check - If enabled, system ROM overrides all cards Address range decode - Determines I/O, ROM, or Extended ROM space SLOTCXROM/SLOTC3ROM check - Determines card vs. system ROM (when INTCXROM off) Card query - Asks the card for data (may return null) Fallback - Returns system ROM or floating bus value Extended ROM Bank Selection: The BankSelect property tracks which slot (1-7) currently owns the $C800-$CFFF extended ROM space. Accessing a slot's ROM ($Cn00-$CnFF) activates that slot; accessing $CFFF disables all extended ROM. Configuration Management: The Slots class implements IConfigurable (via ISlots), enabling complete save/restore of the slot configuration. The metadata format includes: Which cards are installed in which slots (by ID) Each card's configuration metadata (hierarchical inclusion) Current BankSelect state (optional) This allows the entire peripheral configuration to be saved, shared, and restored, including disk images, serial port settings, and other card-specific configurations. Empty slots are omitted from the metadata to keep it concise. Threading: This class is not thread-safe. All methods must be called from the emulator worker thread. Card installation/removal during emulation may cause race conditions. Constructors Slots(ICardFactory, ISystemRomProvider, IFloatingBusProvider, ISystemStatusProvider) Initializes a new instance of the Slots class with all slots empty. public Slots(ICardFactory factory, ISystemRomProvider rom, IFloatingBusProvider floatingBus, ISystemStatusProvider status) Parameters factory ICardFactory The card factory for creating card instances. rom ISystemRomProvider The system ROM provider for $C000-$CFFF range. floatingBus IFloatingBusProvider The floating bus provider for unresponsive addresses. status ISystemStatusProvider The system status provider for soft switch states. Remarks All seven expansion slots (1-7) are initialized with NullCard instances, representing empty slots. Slot 0 is also filled with a NullCard since it's reserved for system use and should never be accessed. The constructor creates independent NullCard instances via Clone() to ensure each slot has its own instance, even though NullCards are stateless. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if any parameter is null. InvalidOperationException Thrown if the card factory cannot create a NullCard for initialization. Properties BankSelect Gets or sets the currently active slot for extended ROM at $C800-$CFFF. public byte BankSelect { get; set; } Property Value byte A value from 0-7 where: 0 = No slot active (extended ROM disabled) 1-7 = That slot's extended ROM is active at $C800-$CFFF Remarks This property implements the Apple IIe's extended ROM bank switching mechanism. The hardware allows only one slot's extended ROM to be visible at $C800-$CFFF at any given time. The active slot is determined by: Reading/writing $Cn00-$CnFF sets BankSelect to slot n Reading/writing $CFFF sets BankSelect to 0 (disabled) The bank selection state is preserved even when INTCXROM is enabled (which overrides extended ROM with system ROM but doesn't clear the selection). This allows the previously selected card's extended ROM to reappear when INTCXROM is disabled. Example: A Disk II controller in slot 6 has boot ROM at $C600-$C6FF and extended driver code at its extended ROM. When the system reads $C600, slot 6's extended ROM becomes active at $C800-$CFFF. Reading $CFFF disables extended ROM. this[ushort] Gets or sets a byte at the specified address using array-style indexing. public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; } Parameters address ushort The address to access, offset by $C000. For example, to access $C600, use 0x0600. Property Value byte The byte value at the specified address (reading or writing). Examples // Read from $C600 byte bootByte = slots[0x0600]; // Write to $C0EC (slot 6, I/O offset $0C) slots[0x00EC] = 0x00; Remarks This indexer provides convenient array-style access to the slots address space, delegating to Read(ushort) for gets and Write(ushort, byte) for sets. Size Gets the size of the address space managed by this slots system. public int Size { get; } Property Value int Always returns 0x1000 (4096 bytes), representing the $C000-$CFFF range. Remarks Although the value indicates $C000-$CFFF (4KB), the slots system only handles $C090-$CFFF (3952 bytes). The range $C000-$C08F contains soft switches and other system I/O that's handled elsewhere (typically by VA2MBus). If Read(ushort) or Write(ushort, byte) is called with an address in the $C000-$C08F range (0x0000-0x008F when offset), an InvalidOperationException will be thrown. Methods ApplyMetadata(string) Applies slot configuration from JSON metadata, restoring installed cards and their configurations. public bool ApplyMetadata(string metadata) Parameters metadata string A JSON metadata string previously obtained from GetMetadata(), or an empty string to clear all slots. Returns bool true if the configuration was successfully applied; false if the metadata was invalid or any card failed to apply its configuration. Examples // Save current configuration string backup = slots.GetMetadata(); // Load a different configuration if (!slots.ApplyMetadata(savedConfiguration)) { // Restore backup on failure slots.ApplyMetadata(backup); } // Clear all slots slots.ApplyMetadata(string.Empty); Remarks This method restores the slot configuration from metadata by: Clearing all slots (removing all cards) Parsing the JSON metadata Installing each card by ID in its designated slot Recursively applying each card's embedded metadata Restoring the BankSelect state Empty String Handling: Passing an empty or whitespace-only string clears all slots, leaving the system with empty slots (all NullCard instances). This represents the default/unconfigured state. Error Handling: If any card fails to install or configure, this method returns false. The slot system may be left in a partial state where some cards are installed and others are not. For transactional behavior, callers should capture the current metadata before applying new metadata to enable rollback. Card Factory Dependency: This method relies on GetCardWithId(int) to create card instances. If a card ID in the metadata is not registered in the factory, that slot will remain empty and the method will return false. GetCardIn(SlotNumber) Retrieves the card currently installed in the specified slot. public ICard GetCardIn(SlotNumber slot) Parameters slot SlotNumber The slot number (1-7) to query. Returns ICard The ICard instance installed in the slot. Returns a GetNullCard() instance if the slot is empty. Remarks This method provides direct access to the card instance for inspection, debugging, or advanced operations. Normal emulator operation doesn't require calling this method since Read(ushort) and Write(ushort, byte) automatically route to the appropriate card based on address. Usage Examples: // Check what's installed in slot 6 ICard card = slots.GetCardIn(SlotNumber.Slot6); Console.WriteLine($"Slot 6: {card.Name}"); // Verify a slot is empty if (slots.GetCardIn(SlotNumber.Slot3).Id == 0) // NullCard.Id == 0 { Console.WriteLine("Slot 3 is empty"); } GetMetadata() Gets the current slot configuration as JSON metadata. public string GetMetadata() Returns string A JSON string containing the configuration of all installed cards and their metadata, or an empty string if serialization fails. Remarks This method captures the current state of all expansion slots, including which cards are installed and their individual configurations. The metadata uses JSON format with the following structure: { "version": 1, "bankSelect": 6, "slots": [ { "slotNumber": 6, "cardId": 1, "cardName": "Disk II Controller", "metadata": "{...card-specific configuration...}" } ] } Hierarchical Configuration: The slots system uses the inclusive approach to hierarchical configuration. Each installed card's metadata (obtained via GetMetadata()) is embedded within the slot configuration. This keeps the entire peripheral configuration self-contained and portable. Empty Slots: Empty slots (containing NullCard) are omitted from the metadata to keep it concise. On restoration, any slot not mentioned in the metadata is left empty. BankSelect State: The current BankSelect value is included in the metadata. While not strictly necessary for configuration (it's runtime state), including it allows for precise state restoration during debugging or save states. InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) Installs a peripheral card in the specified slot using its numeric card ID. public void InstallCard(int id, SlotNumber slot) Parameters id int The unique numeric identifier of the card type to install (see Id). slot SlotNumber The slot number (1-7) where the card should be installed. Remarks This method uses the ICardFactory to create a new instance of the specified card type via GetCardWithId(int). The card is cloned from a registered prototype, ensuring each slot gets an independent instance. If a card is already installed in the specified slot, it is replaced by the new card. No state from the previous card is preserved. Common card IDs: 0 = NullCard (empty slot) 1 = Disk II Controller 2 = Super Serial Card Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the card factory cannot create a card with the specified ID. See Also InstallCard(string, SlotNumber) RemoveCard(SlotNumber) InstallCard(string, SlotNumber) Installs a peripheral card in the specified slot using its human-readable name. public void InstallCard(string name, SlotNumber slot) Parameters name string The name of the card type to install (see Name). slot SlotNumber The slot number (1-7) where the card should be installed. Remarks This method provides a more user-friendly alternative to InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) by accepting the card's display name instead of its numeric ID. The card name must exactly match the value returned by Name (case-sensitive). Example usage: slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6); slots.InstallCard("Super Serial Card", SlotNumber.Slot2); If a card is already installed in the specified slot, it is replaced by the new card. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the card factory cannot create a card with the specified name. See Also InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) RemoveCard(SlotNumber) IsEmpty(SlotNumber) Determines whether the specified slot is empty (contains a NullCard). public bool IsEmpty(SlotNumber slot) Parameters slot SlotNumber The slot number (1-7) to check. Returns bool true if the slot contains a GetNullCard() (empty slot); false if the slot contains an actual peripheral card. Remarks This is a convenience method that checks whether the card in the specified slot has an Id of 0 (the NullCard identifier). It provides a more readable alternative to manually checking the card ID. Usage Examples: // Clear alternative to checking card ID if (slots.IsEmpty(SlotNumber.Slot6)) { slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6); } // List all installed cards for (int i = 1; i <= 7; i++) { var slot = (SlotNumber)(i - 1); if (!slots.IsEmpty(slot)) { Console.WriteLine($"Slot {i}: {slots.GetCardIn(slot).Name}"); } } This method is equivalent to: bool IsEmpty(SlotNumber slot) => GetCardIn(slot).Id == 0; See Also GetCardIn(SlotNumber) RemoveCard(SlotNumber) Read(ushort) Reads a byte from the slots address space ($C090-$CFFF). public byte Read(ushort address) Parameters address ushort The address to read, offset by $C000. For example, to read from $C600, pass 0x0600. Valid range: 0x0090-0x0FFF. Returns byte The byte value from the responding device (card, system ROM, or floating bus). Remarks Address Decoding Logic: $C090-$C0FF (Card I/O Space): If INTCXROM is enabled, returns system ROM. Otherwise, extracts slot number from address bits 4-6 and calls the card's ReadIO(byte). Returns floating bus if card returns null. This range never modifies BankSelect. $C100-$C7FF (Card ROM Space): If INTCXROM is enabled, returns system ROM. Otherwise, checks SLOTCXROM (or SLOTC3ROM for slot 3) to determine card vs. system ROM. If using card ROM, sets BankSelect to the slot number and calls ReadRom(byte). Returns floating bus if card returns null, or system ROM if soft switches indicate system ROM. $C800-$CFFF (Extended ROM Space): If INTCXROM is enabled, returns system ROM. Otherwise, checks BankSelect to determine which card's extended ROM is active. If BankSelect is non-zero, calls that card's ReadExtendedRom(ushort). Returns floating bus if BankSelect is 0 or if card returns null. Special Behavior at $CFFF: Reading from $CFFF (0x0FFF) disables extended ROM by setting BankSelect to 0. The read still returns the byte from the currently active card (if any) before being disabled. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the address is outside the valid range ($C090-$CFFF / 0x0090-0x0FFF). See Also Write(ushort, byte) BankSelect RemoveCard(SlotNumber) Removes the card from the specified slot, replacing it with an empty slot (NullCard). public void RemoveCard(SlotNumber slot) Parameters slot SlotNumber The slot number (1-7) to clear. Remarks This method replaces the card in the specified slot with a GetNullCard() instance. The NullCard returns null for all read operations and no-ops for all writes, causing the slot to behave as if it's empty (returning floating bus values for reads). Any state maintained by the removed card is lost. If the removed card was the active extended ROM slot (see BankSelect), the extended ROM remains active but will return floating bus values since NullCard returns null. Note: This method cannot fail due to the card being "in use" since the emulator doesn't maintain references to installed cards outside the slots array. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the card factory cannot create a NullCard replacement. See Also InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) InstallCard(string, SlotNumber) Write(ushort, byte) Writes a byte to the slots address space ($C090-$CFFF). public void Write(ushort address, byte val) Parameters address ushort The address to write, offset by $C000. For example, to write to $C600, pass 0x0600. Valid range: 0x0090-0x0FFF. val byte The byte value to write. Remarks Address Decoding Logic: $C090-$C0FF (Card I/O Space): If INTCXROM is enabled, writes to system ROM (usually a no-op). Otherwise, extracts slot number from address bits 4-6 and calls the card's WriteIO(byte, byte). Cards typically use this space for control registers, command triggers, and data output. This range never modifies BankSelect. $C100-$C7FF (Card ROM Space): If INTCXROM is enabled, writes to system ROM (usually a no-op). Otherwise, checks SLOTCXROM (or SLOTC3ROM for slot 3) to determine card vs. system ROM. If using card ROM, sets BankSelect to the slot number and calls WriteRom(byte, byte). Most cards treat this as a no-op since ROM is read-only, but some cards may implement writeable RAM in this space. $C800-$CFFF (Extended ROM Space): If INTCXROM is enabled, writes to system ROM (usually a no-op). Otherwise, if BankSelect is non-zero, calls that card's WriteExtendedRom(ushort, byte). If BankSelect is 0, the write is silently ignored (no floating bus effect). Special Behavior at $CFFF: Writing to $CFFF (0x0FFF) disables extended ROM by setting BankSelect to 0. The write still reaches the currently active card (if any) before being disabled. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the address is outside the valid range ($C090-$CFFF / 0x0090-0x0FFF). See Also Read(ushort) BankSelect

Examples: 80STORE ($C000/$C001), RAMRD ($C002/$C003), TEXT ($C050/$C051), PAGE2 ($C054/$C055). Inherits change-counting functionality to track how often each switch changes state, useful for debugging and performance analysis.
