api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.SoftSwitches.SoftSw.title: Enum SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.SoftSwitches.SoftSw.summary: Enum SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Identifies specific Apple IIe soft switches for type-safe access. public enum SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId Fields AltChar = 7 ALTCHAR switch ($C00E/$C00F). Selects alternate character set for text mode. AltZp = 4 ALTZP switch ($C008/$C009). When enabled, zero page ($0000-$01FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) are mapped to auxiliary memory. An0 = 12 Annunciator 0 ($C058/$C059). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control. An1 = 13 Annunciator 1 ($C05A/$C05B). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control. An2 = 14 Annunciator 2 ($C05C/$C05D). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control. An3 = 15 Annunciator 3 ($C05E/$C05F). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control (commonly used for double hi-res mode). Bank1 = 16 BANK1 switch. Selects which bank of auxiliary memory is active for the language card area ($D000-$FFFF). HiRes = 11 HIRES switch ($C056/$C057). When enabled, selects high-resolution graphics mode; when disabled, selects low-resolution graphics mode. HighRead = 18 HIGHREAD switch. Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF come from RAM or ROM. HighWrite = 17 HIGHWRITE switch. Controls write protection for the language card RAM in the $D000-$FFFF range. IntCxRom = 3 INTCXROM switch ($C006/$C007). When enabled, accesses to $C100-$CFFF use internal ROM instead of peripheral card ROMs. Mixed = 9 MIXED switch ($C052/$C053). When enabled, displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom of the screen. Page2 = 10 PAGE2 switch ($C054/$C055). Selects display page (page 1 or page 2) and may affect auxiliary memory access depending on 80STORE state. PreWrite = 19 PREWRITE switch. Pre-write state for language card write protection. Two consecutive reads of certain addresses are required to enable writing. RamRd = 1 RAMRD switch ($C002/$C003). Controls whether reads from certain memory ranges come from main or auxiliary memory. RamWrt = 2 RAMWRT switch ($C004/$C005). Controls whether writes to certain memory ranges go to main or auxiliary memory. SlotC3Rom = 5 SLOTC3ROM switch ($C00A/$C00B). When enabled, accesses to $C300-$C3FF use internal ROM instead of slot 3 card ROM. Store80 = 0 80STORE switch ($C000/$C001). When enabled, affects whether PAGE2 switch controls auxiliary memory or video display page selection. Text = 8 TEXT switch ($C050/$C051). When enabled, display shows text mode; when disabled, shows graphics mode. Vid80 = 6 80VID switch ($C00C/$C00D). Enables 80-column video mode when set. Remarks Each enum value corresponds to a specific Apple IIe soft switch that controls memory mapping, video modes, ROM selection, or annunciators.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.SoftSwitches.html.title: Class SoftSwitches | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.SoftSwitches.html.summary: Class SoftSwitches Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Manages all Apple IIe soft switches and notifies responders when switches change. public sealed class SoftSwitches Inheritance object SoftSwitches Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Responder Pattern: Components register as ISoftSwitchResponder to receive notifications when switches change. This decouples switch management from the components that react to changes (MemoryPool, video renderer, etc.). Switch Categories: Memory Mapping (RAMRD, RAMWRT, ALTZP, 80STORE, BANK1), Video Mode (TEXT, MIXED, HIRES, PAGE2, 80VID, ALTCHAR), ROM Selection (INTCXROM, SLOTC3ROM), Annunciators (AN0-AN3). Constructors SoftSwitches() Initializes a new instance of the SoftSwitches class with all switches set to their default states. public SoftSwitches() Remarks All switches are initialized to false (off) except INTCXROM which defaults to true. This matches the Apple IIe power-on state where internal ROMs are enabled by default. Methods AddResponder(ISoftSwitchResponder) Registers a component to receive notifications when soft switches change. public void AddResponder(ISoftSwitchResponder responder) Parameters responder ISoftSwitchResponder The responder to add. Must implement ISoftSwitchResponder. Remarks Responders receive immediate callbacks when switches change via the TriggerResponder(SoftSwitchId, bool) method. Typical responders include MemoryPool (for memory remapping) and video renderers (for display mode changes). DumpSoftSwitchStatus(string) Writes the current state of all soft switches to the debug output. public void DumpSoftSwitchStatus(string header = "") Parameters header string Optional header text to display before the switch status list. If provided, each switch status line will be indented. Remarks Output format: "SwitchName: On/Off (Changes: count)" Useful for debugging switch state during emulation and tracking switch activity patterns. Get(SoftSwitchId) Retrieves the current state of a specific soft switch. public bool Get(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id) Parameters id SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId The identifier of the switch to query. Returns bool True if the switch is on (enabled), false if off (disabled) or not found. GetSwitchList() Returns a snapshot of all switches with their current state and change counts. public List<(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id, bool value)> GetSwitchList() Returns List<(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id, bool value)> A list of tuples containing switch ID and current valuefor each switch. Remarks Useful for diagnostics, save state serialization, and UI display of switch status. The change count indicates how many times each switch has toggled since reset. ResetAllSwitches() Resets all soft switches to their default power-on state. public void ResetAllSwitches() Remarks Default state: All switches off except INTCXROM which is on (matching Apple IIe power-on). All registered responders are notified of the state changes to ensure memory mappings and video modes are properly initialized. Set(SoftSwitchId, bool) Sets the state of a specific soft switch and notifies all registered responders. public void Set(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id, bool value) Parameters id SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId The identifier of the switch to modify. value bool The new state for the switch (true = on, false = off). Remarks This method updates the switch state and immediately triggers responder callbacks, which may cause memory remapping or video mode changes. The change counter for the switch is automatically incremented if the value changes.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.SystemRamSelector.h.title: Class SystemRamSelector | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.SystemRamSelector.h.summary: Class SystemRamSelector Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll public class SystemRamSelector : ISystemRamSelector, IMemory, IDirectMemoryPoolReader Inheritance object SystemRamSelector Implements ISystemRamSelector IMemory IDirectMemoryPoolReader Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors SystemRamSelector(ISystemRam, ISystemRam?, IFloatingBusProvider, ISystemStatusProvider) public SystemRamSelector(ISystemRam mainRam, ISystemRam? auxRam, IFloatingBusProvider floatingBus, ISystemStatusProvider status) Parameters mainRam ISystemRam auxRam ISystemRam floatingBus IFloatingBusProvider status ISystemStatusProvider Properties this[ushort] public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; } Parameters address ushort Property Value byte Size public int Size { get; } Property Value int Methods Read(ushort) public byte Read(ushort address) Parameters address ushort Returns byte ReadRawAux(int) Reads a byte from the auxiliary memory pool, bypassing all bus mapping and soft switches. public byte ReadRawAux(int address) Parameters address int The physical address to read from ($0000-$FFFF). Note that for the language card region, bank 1 ($D000-$DFFF logical) is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 ($D000-$DFFF logical) is at $D000-$DFFF physical. Returns byte The byte value at the specified physical address in auxiliary memory, or 0 if the address is not available (e.g., addresses where no auxiliary RAM exists). Remarks This method reads directly from the auxiliary 64KB memory pool without any address translation, auxiliary memory selection, or bank switching logic. It always reads from auxiliary memory, never main memory, and ignores all soft switch states (RAMRD, RAMWRT, 80STORE, etc.). The Apple IIe's auxiliary memory is primarily used for 80-column text mode, double hi-res graphics, and extended memory for programs. Use this method when you need direct access to the auxiliary memory bank, such as when rendering 80-column text or double hi-res graphics. ReadRawMain(int) Reads a byte from the main memory pool, bypassing all bus mapping and soft switches. public byte ReadRawMain(int address) Parameters address int The physical address to read from ($0000-$FFFF). Note that for the language card region, bank 1 ($D000-$DFFF logical) is physically located at $C000-$CFFF, while bank 2 ($D000-$DFFF logical) is at $D000-$DFFF physical. Returns byte The byte value at the specified physical address in main memory, or 0 if the address is not available (e.g., ROM regions with no RAM underneath). Remarks This method reads directly from the main 64KB memory pool without any address translation, auxiliary memory selection, or bank switching logic. It always reads from main memory, never auxiliary memory, and ignores all soft switch states (RAMRD, RAMWRT, 80STORE, etc.). Use this when you need to access the actual physical memory layout, such as when rendering video from specific memory pages or debugging memory contents. Write(ushort, byte) public void Write(ushort address, byte data) Parameters address ushort data byte

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.VA2M.html.title: Class VA2M | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.VA2M.html.summary: Class VA2M Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll Main Apple IIe emulator orchestrator coordinating CPU, bus, memory, and timing. public class VA2M : IDisposable Inheritance object VA2M Implements IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks ⚠️ PLANNED FOR REFACTORING: This class will be refactored to separate concerns (timing, input, state publishing) into dedicated services. See Pandowdy.EmuCore/_docs_/VA2MBus-Refactoring-Notes.md for planned architecture. Name Origin: "VA2M" = "Virtual Apple II Machinator" - the machine orchestrator that brings together all emulator subsystems. Threading Model: Emulator Thread: Single-threaded CPU execution (Clock/RunAsync loop) External Threads: UI/input threads enqueue commands via InjectKey(), etc. Flash Timer: Separate timer thread toggles cursor at ~2.1 Hz Synchronization: Commands processed at frame boundaries (thread-safe) Execution Modes: Clock(): Single-cycle execution, useful for debugging/stepping RunAsync(): Continuous batched execution, normal operation mode Constructors VA2M(IEmulatorState, IFrameProvider, ISystemStatusProvider, IAppleIIBus, MemoryPool, IFrameGenerator) Initializes a new instance of the VA2M emulator. public VA2M(IEmulatorState stateSink, IFrameProvider frameSink, ISystemStatusProvider statusProvider, IAppleIIBus bus, MemoryPool memoryPool, IFrameGenerator frameGenerator) Parameters stateSink IEmulatorState State provider for publishing emulator state snapshots. frameSink IFrameProvider Frame provider for publishing rendered video frames. statusProvider ISystemStatusProvider System status provider for soft switch states. bus IAppleIIBus System bus coordinating CPU, memory, and I/O. memoryPool MemoryPool Memory pool managing 128KB Apple IIe memory. frameGenerator IFrameGenerator Frame generator for rendering video output. Remarks Initialization Sequence: Store dependency references (all required) Load embedded Apple IIe ROM from resources Subscribe to VBlank event from bus (if VA2MBus) Start flash timer for cursor blinking ROM Loading: The Apple IIe Enhanced ROM (16KB) is embedded as a resource and automatically loaded during construction. This ROM contains the Monitor, Applesoft BASIC, and peripheral firmware. VBlank Event: If the bus is a VA2MBus, the OnVBlank handler is registered to trigger frame rendering and flash state updates at ~60 Hz. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if any parameter is null. Properties Bus Gets the system bus coordinating CPU, memory, and I/O access. public IAppleIIBus Bus { get; } Property Value IAppleIIBus MemoryPool Gets the memory pool managing the 128KB Apple IIe memory space. public MemoryPool MemoryPool { get; } Property Value MemoryPool SystemClock Gets the total number of CPU cycles executed since last reset. public ulong SystemClock { get; } Property Value ulong TargetHz Gets or sets the target CPU frequency in Hz. public double TargetHz { get; set; } Property Value double Remarks Default is 1,023,000 Hz (1.023 MHz), the Apple IIe's clock speed. Can be adjusted for testing or to match different Apple II models. ThrottleEnabled Gets or sets whether throttling is enabled to maintain Apple IIe speed. public bool ThrottleEnabled { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks When true, the emulator runs at ~1.023 MHz (Apple IIe speed). When false, runs as fast as possible (useful for loading programs, debugging). Methods Clock() Advance one system clock (one CPU/bus cycle). If throttling is enabled, the call will delay to keep approx TargetHz. Suitable for simple loops. public void Clock() Remarks Execution Sequence: Process pending cross-thread commands (ProcessPending) Execute one bus clock cycle (CPU + memory + I/O) Increment throttle cycle counter Apply throttling delay if enabled (ThrottleOneCycle) Publish emulator state snapshot Use Cases: Single-step debugging (execute one instruction at a time) Simple synchronous execution loops Testing and verification scenarios Performance Note: For continuous operation, use RunAsync(CancellationToken, double) instead as it batches cycles to reduce per-cycle overhead. Dispose() Releases all resources used by the VA2M emulator instance. public void Dispose() Remarks Cleanup Sequence: Stop and dispose flash timer (~2.1 Hz cursor blink timer) Clear pending command queue to release enqueued actions Dispose bus (includes VBlank event cleanup) Suppress finalization (no unmanaged resources) Thread Safety: Should be called from the thread that owns the emulator instance (typically the UI thread after stopping emulation). Do not call while RunAsync() is executing. MemoryPool Note: MemoryPool disposal is currently commented out as it may be shared across multiple emulator instances or have external ownership. This should be revisited if MemoryPool lifetime is clarified. CPU Note: The legacy 6502.NET CPU library does not implement IDisposable, so no explicit CPU cleanup is needed. The CPU instance is managed by the Bus. GenerateStatusData() Enqueues generation of a system status snapshot containing soft switch and button states. public void GenerateStatusData() Remarks Purpose: Triggers creation of a comprehensive system status snapshot that includes all soft switches (memory mapping, video modes, ROM, annunciators) and pushbutton states. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe. It enqueues the status generation command which will be executed on the emulator thread via Pandowdy.EmuCore.VA2M.BuildStatusData(). Typical Usage: Called periodically (e.g., at frame boundaries or on-demand) to update UI displays showing soft switch states, debug panels, or system status indicators. Snapshot Contents: See Pandowdy.EmuCore.VA2M.BuildStatusData() for details on what data is captured in the status snapshot. InjectKey(byte) Inject a keyboard value into the machine as if a key was latched at $C000. High bit must be set. Cleared by access of $C010. public void InjectKey(byte ascii) Parameters ascii byte ASCII character code (0-127). High bit will be set automatically. Remarks Apple IIe Keyboard Protocol: The Apple IIe keyboard latch is a memory-mapped register at $C000. When a key is pressed, the ASCII value with bit 7 set appears at this address. Reading $C010 (KBDSTRB) clears bit 7, indicating the key has been read. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe. It enqueues the key injection command which will be executed on the emulator thread at the next ProcessPending() call. This allows UI threads to inject keyboard input without race conditions. ASCII Codes: 0x20-0x7E: Standard printable ASCII characters 0x0D (13): Return/Enter key 0x08 (8): Backspace (sometimes) 0x15 (21): Right arrow (Apple IIe convention) 0x1B (27): Escape Implementation: The method automatically sets bit 7 (ORs with 0x80) to match Apple IIe hardware behavior. The command is enqueued and executed via Bus.SetKeyValue() on the emulator thread. Reset() Reset machine and system clock. public void Reset() Remarks Full System Reset (Power Cycle): This method performs a complete hardware reset equivalent to powering off and on the Apple IIe. Operations Performed: Reset CPU registers (PC loaded from $FFFC/$FFFD, SP = $FF) Reset all soft switches to power-on state Reset memory bank mappings Clear keyboard latch Reset cycle counter to zero Restart throttle stopwatch Reset performance measurement counters Difference from UserReset: Reset() is a cold boot that initializes everything. UserReset() is a warm reset that preserves memory contents and only resets the CPU (Ctrl+Reset behavior). Thread Safety: Should be called from the emulator thread. For cross-thread reset, enqueue via command queue. RunAsync(CancellationToken, double) Run the emulator asynchronously with batched cycles and time slices. Batches cycles per tick (e.g.,1 ms or 60 Hz) to reduce overhead of per-cycle waits. When ThrottleEnabled is true, pacing uses the periodic timer to approximate TargetHz. When false, runs fast batches without waiting. public Task RunAsync(CancellationToken ct, double ticksPerSecond = 1000) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token to stop the runner. ticksPerSecond double Time slice frequency. Use 1000 for 1ms slices or 60 for video-frame pacing. Returns Task A task that completes when the cancellation token is triggered or the emulator stops. Remarks Throttled Mode (ThrottleEnabled = true): Uses PeriodicTimer to wait for each tick (e.g., 1ms or ~16.67ms for 60 Hz) Executes calculated number of cycles per tick (TargetHz / ticksPerSecond) Accumulates fractional cycles to prevent drift over time Processes pending commands before each batch Publishes state after each batch Fast Mode (ThrottleEnabled = false): Executes 10,000 cycle batches as fast as possible No timer delays (runs full CPU speed) Yields with Task.Delay(0) to remain responsive Useful for loading programs quickly or testing Recommended ticksPerSecond Values: 1000: 1ms time slices, ~1,023 cycles/tick (balanced) 60: ~16.67ms slices, ~17,050 cycles/tick (matches VBlank) 100: 10ms slices, ~10,230 cycles/tick (less frequent) Fractional Cycle Accumulation: The algorithm tracks fractional cycles with a carry variable to prevent cumulative drift. For example, with ticksPerSecond=1000, each tick should execute 1023 cycles, but the fractional 0.0 carry is accumulated so over time the average is exactly 1,023,000 Hz. Cancellation: The loop checks the cancellation token after each batch and breaks cleanly. OperationCanceledException is caught and handled gracefully. ConfigureAwait(false): Used to avoid capturing synchronization context, improving performance and preventing potential deadlocks in UI scenarios. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if ticksPerSecond is zero or negative. SetPushButton(byte, bool) Sets the state of an Apple IIe pushbutton (game controller button). public void SetPushButton(byte num, bool pressed) Parameters num byte Button number (0-2). Button 0 is typically button 0, buttons 1-2 are paddle buttons. pressed bool True if button is pressed, false if released. Remarks Apple IIe Pushbutton Hardware: The Apple IIe has three pushbutton inputs typically used for game controllers (joysticks/paddles). These are read from I/O addresses $C061-$C063. Button Mapping: Button 0 ($C061): Typically joystick/paddle button 0 Button 1 ($C062): Typically joystick/paddle button 1 Button 2 ($C063): Typically joystick/paddle button 2 Reading Buttons: Software reads the button state by checking bit 7 of the corresponding I/O address. If bit 7 is set, the button is pressed. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe. It enqueues the button state change which will be executed on the emulator thread at the next ProcessPending() call. UserReset() Performs a warm reset (Ctrl+Reset) without clearing memory or resetting cycle counters. public void UserReset() Remarks Warm Reset Behavior: Simulates pressing Ctrl+Reset on the Apple IIe, which resets the CPU but preserves RAM contents and continues timing. Operations Performed: Reset CPU registers (PC loaded from $FFFC/$FFFD, SP reset) Memory contents preserved (RAM, soft switches mostly unchanged) Cycle counter continues (not reset) Throttle timing continues (not restarted) Use Cases: Break out of infinite loop without losing program in memory Return to Monitor/BASIC prompt from running program Recover from program hang while preserving state Difference from Reset: Reset() is a cold boot (power cycle). UserReset() is a warm reset that preserves memory and timing state. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe. It enqueues the reset operation which will be executed on the emulator thread at the next instruction boundary (via ProcessPending). This ensures the reset doesn't interrupt a CPU instruction in progress, maintaining 6502 atomic instruction guarantees. Implementation Note: The reset command is enqueued and will be processed at the next safe point (instruction boundary) on the emulator thread. This prevents race conditions and undefined behavior from resetting the CPU mid-instruction.

api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.VA2MBus.html.title: Class VA2MBus | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.EmuCore.VA2MBus.html.summary: Class VA2MBus Namespace Pandowdy.EmuCore Assembly Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll VA2M-specific system bus that coordinates CPU, memory, and I/O operations for Apple IIe emulation. public sealed class VA2MBus : IAppleIIBus, IBus, IDisposable Inheritance object VA2MBus Implements IAppleIIBus IBus IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Responsibilities: CPU connection and cycle execution Memory read/write routing to MemoryPool I/O space address decoding ($C000-$CFFF) Soft switch management and coordination Language card banking logic Keyboard input handling Game controller pushbutton management VBlank timing and event generation (~60 Hz) Threading Model: All public methods (Connect, CpuRead/Write, Clock, Reset) must be called ONLY from the emulator worker thread. The VBlank event is raised on the emulator thread; UI subscribers MUST marshal to UI thread via dispatcher. VBlank Timing: Emits VBlank event every 17,063 cycles (~60 Hz at 1.023 MHz), matching the Apple IIe NTSC vertical blanking interval. The VBlank blackout period lasts 4,550 cycles during which RD_VERTBLANK_ ($C019) reads as $80. Disposal: After disposal, no further events are raised and Clock() becomes a no-op. Proper disposal prevents event handler leaks and ensures clean shutdown. ⚠️ PLANNED FOR REFACTORING: See Pandowdy.EmuCore/_docs_/VA2MBus-Refactoring-Notes.md for planned separation of concerns (slot bus system, floating bus emulation, etc.). Constructors VA2MBus(MemoryPool, ISystemStatusProvider, ICpu) Initializes a new instance of the VA2MBus class. public VA2MBus(MemoryPool mempool, ISystemStatusProvider statusProvider, ICpu cpu) Parameters mempool MemoryPool Memory pool managing 128KB Apple IIe memory space. statusProvider ISystemStatusProvider System status provider implementing ISoftSwitchResponder for status updates. cpu ICpu CPU instance (6502 emulator) to connect to this bus. Remarks Initialization Sequence: Store dependency references Initialize I/O read handler dictionary (InitIoReadHandlers) Initialize I/O write handler dictionary (InitIoWriteHandlers) Register MemoryPool as soft switch responder (for memory remapping) Register SystemStatusProvider as soft switch responder (for UI updates) I/O Handler Dictionaries: The bus maintains two dictionaries mapping I/O addresses to handler functions. This provides efficient dispatch and clear organization of I/O space behavior. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if any parameter is null. main RAM ($C004 write). public const ushort WRMAINRAM_ = 49156 Field Value ushort Properties Cpu Gets the CPU instance connected to this bus. public ICpu Cpu { get; } Property Value ICpu RAM Gets the RAM (MemoryPool) for direct memory access. public IMemory RAM { get; } Property Value IMemory Remarks Primarily used for testing and direct memory inspection. Normal CPU access should go through CpuRead/CpuWrite which handle I/O space routing. Switches Gets the soft switches managing memory mapping, video modes, ROM selection, and annunciators. public SoftSwitches Switches { get; } Property Value SoftSwitches Remarks Provides access to the soft switch collection for querying states, registering responders, and debugging. The MemoryPool and SystemStatusProvider are automatically registered as responders during construction. SystemClockCounter Gets the total number of CPU cycles executed since last reset. public ulong SystemClockCounter { get; } Property Value ulong Methods Clock() Executes one CPU clock cycle and updates VBlank timing. public void Clock() Remarks Execution Sequence: Execute one CPU instruction (CPU.Clock) Increment system clock counter Decrement VBlank blackout counter Check if VBlank cycle reached, fire event if so VBlank Timing: When _systemClock reaches _nextVblankCycle (initially 12,480, then 29,510, 46,540, ...), the VBlank event is fired and _nextVblankCycle is advanced by CyclesPerVBlank (17,030). The VBlank blackout counter is reset to 4,550 cycles. Frame Cycle Calculation: Frame 0: VBlank at cycle 12,480 (scanline 192) Frame 1: VBlank at cycle 29,510 (12,480 + 17,030) Frame 2: VBlank at cycle 46,540 (29,510 + 17,030) ...and so on Catch-Up Logic: If the emulator runs fast (unthrottled batches), multiple VBlank cycles may be skipped. The do-while loop ensures _nextVblankCycle catches up to _systemClock, preventing event spam while maintaining proper phase alignment. 80-Column Synchronization: By firing VBlank at cycle 12,480 (not 0), we maintain synchronization with Apple IIe 80-column firmware which rapidly toggles PAGE2 during visible scanlines and relies on VBlank flag ($C019) for timing. Disposal Safety: If the bus has been disposed, Clock() returns immediately without executing CPU or firing events. Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only. VBlank event subscribers must marshal to UI thread if needed. Connect(CPU) Legacy CPU connection method. Not supported in VA2MBus. public void Connect(CPU _) Parameters _ CPU CPU instance (ignored). Remarks VA2MBus uses constructor injection for the CPU connection. The Connect method is deprecated and should not be called. This method exists only for IAppleIIBus interface compatibility. Exceptions NotSupportedException Always thrown. VA2MBus requires CPU in constructor. CpuRead(ushort, bool) Reads a byte from memory or I/O space, routing to appropriate handler. public byte CpuRead(ushort address, bool readOnly = false) Parameters address ushort 16-bit address to read from ($0000-$FFFF). readOnly bool If true, indicates a non-mutating read (not currently used). Returns byte Byte value from memory or I/O space. Remarks Routing: $C000-$C0FF: I/O space (ReadFromIOSpace) All other addresses: MemoryPool.Read (RAM/ROM) Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the bus has been disposed. CpuWrite(ushort, byte) Writes a byte to memory or I/O space, routing to appropriate handler. public void CpuWrite(ushort address, byte data) Parameters address ushort 16-bit address to write to ($0000-$FFFF). data byte Byte value to write. Remarks Routing: $C000-$C0FF: I/O space (WriteToIOSpace) All other addresses: MemoryPool.Write (RAM, respecting write protection) Write Protection: The MemoryPool enforces write protection based on soft switch states (ROM areas, language card write enable, etc.). Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the bus has been disposed. Dispose() Disposes the bus, cleaning up resources and preventing further operation. public void Dispose() Remarks Cleanup Operations: Set _disposed flag to true Clear VBlank event subscribers (prevent event handler leaks) Post-Disposal Behavior: Clock() becomes a no-op (returns immediately) CpuRead/CpuWrite throw ObjectDisposedException VBlank event is never fired again Idempotent: Multiple calls to Dispose() are safe (no-op after first call). Thread Safety: Should be called from the thread that owns the bus instance (typically after stopping emulation). GetPushButton(int) Gets the current state of a pushbutton. public bool GetPushButton(int num) Parameters num int Button number (0-2). Returns bool True if button is pressed, false if released or invalid button number. Remarks Button states are readable at I/O addresses $C061-$C063 with bit 7 set when pressed. Reset() Performs a full system reset (power cycle). public void Reset() Remarks Reset Operations: Reset memory ranges (MemoryPool.ResetRanges) Reset all soft switches to power-on state Reset CPU (PC loaded from $FFFC/$FFFD, SP = $FF) Reset system clock to zero Reset VBlank cycle counter Difference from UserReset: Reset() is a cold boot that clears everything. UserReset() is a warm reset that preserves more state. Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the bus has been disposed. SetKeyValue(byte) Sets the keyboard latch value as if a key was pressed. public void SetKeyValue(byte key) Parameters key byte ASCII key value with bit 7 set (indicating key available). Remarks Keyboard Latch: The Apple IIe keyboard latch appears at $C000. When a key is pressed, the ASCII value with bit 7 set appears at this address. Software reads $C010 (KEYSTRB) to clear bit 7, indicating the key has been read. Thread Safety: Should be called only from the emulator thread. External threads should enqueue via VA2M.InjectKey(). SetPushButton(int, bool) Sets the state of a pushbutton. public void SetPushButton(int num, bool pressed) Parameters num int Button number (0-2). pressed bool True if button is pressed, false if released. Remarks Thread Safety: Should be called only from the emulator thread. External threads should enqueue via VA2M.SetPushButton(). Invalid button numbers are silently ignored. UserReset() Performs a warm reset (Ctrl+Reset) without fully clearing state. public void UserReset() Remarks Reset Operations: Reset all soft switches to power-on state Reset memory ranges (MemoryPool.ResetRanges) Reset CPU (PC loaded from $FFFC/$FFFD) Reset system clock to zero Reset VBlank cycle counter Difference from Reset: In current implementation, UserReset() and Reset() are nearly identical. Future enhancements may preserve more state during UserReset (e.g., RAM contents, some soft switches) to better match Apple IIe Ctrl+Reset behavior. Thread Context: Must be called from emulator thread only. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the bus has been disposed. Events VBlank Event raised every ~60 Hz (17,063 cycles) to signal vertical blanking interval. public event EventHandler? VBlank Event Type EventHandler Remarks Timing: Fires at 1,023,000 Hz / 17,063 cycles ≈ 60.02 Hz (NTSC). Thread Context: Always raised on the emulator thread. UI subscribers must marshal to UI thread via Dispatcher.BeginInvoke or similar. Use Cases: Frame rendering synchronization Cursor blink timing Status snapshot generation Audio buffer flushing

object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides hardware-accurate rendering of Apple IIe video modes including text, low-res graphics, hi-res graphics, and mixed modes with authentic NTSC color artifact simulation or monochrome display. Rendering Features: NTSC color artifact emulation (16 colors from 4-bit patterns) Monochrome mode (green/amber phosphor simulation) Scanline effect for CRT authenticity Aspect ratio preservation with letterboxing Double-scanline rendering (192 → 384 vertical resolution) Keyboard Handling: Captures keyboard input and translates it to Apple IIe key codes, including support for control characters, arrow keys, and caps lock emulation. Frame Source: Receives rendered frames from IFrameProvider (560x192 pixels) and scales them to display resolution (563x384 with padding). Performance: Uses unsafe pointer operations for fast pixel writes during NTSC color generation and scanline rendering. Constructors Apple2Display() Initializes a new instance of the Apple2Display control. public Apple2Display() Remarks Initialization: Creates checkerboard placeholder bitmap Enables keyboard focus for input handling Registers event handlers for keyboard input Sets up focus change visualization Keyboard Events: Registers handlers for KeyDown, KeyUp, and TextInput to capture and translate keyboard input to Apple IIe key codes. Fields BitmapProperty Styled property for the bitmap being displayed. public static readonly StyledProperty<Bitmap?> BitmapProperty Field Value StyledProperty<Bitmap> BrightFringeValue Bright fringe alpha value for color artifact anti-aliasing. public const byte BrightFringeValue = 240 Field Value byte ReducedFringeValue Reduced fringe alpha value for softer color artifact edges. public const byte ReducedFringeValue = 176 Field Value byte Properties ActiveBitPlane Gets or sets which bit plane to render (for debugging double-hires modes). public int ActiveBitPlane { get; set; } Property Value int Bit plane index (0 for main, 1 for auxiliary). Remarks The Apple IIe can use both main and auxiliary memory for double hi-res graphics (560x192 resolution). This property allows selecting which plane to visualize. Typical Values: 0: Main memory bit plane (normal operation) 1: Auxiliary memory bit plane (for debugging or special effects) Bitmap Gets or sets the bitmap being displayed. public Bitmap? Bitmap { get; set; } Property Value Bitmap WriteableBitmap containing the rendered Apple IIe screen, or null. DefringeMixedText Gets or sets whether to defringe mixed text area in mixed graphics mode. public bool DefringeMixedText { get; set; } Property Value bool True to render bottom 4 text lines in monochrome, false for full color. Remarks Mixed mode displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom (lines 20-23). Color artifacts on text can reduce readability. When enabled, the bottom 4 text lines (y >= 160) are rendered in monochrome for improved clarity while keeping graphics in color. ForceMono Gets or sets whether to force monochrome (green/amber) display mode. public bool ForceMono { get; set; } Property Value bool True to render in monochrome, false for NTSC color. Remarks When true, all output is rendered in monochrome regardless of video mode. Simulates green or amber phosphor monitors common with the Apple IIe. ShowScanLines Gets or sets whether to show CRT scanline effect. public bool ShowScanLines { get; set; } Property Value bool True to show scanlines (3/4 brightness on odd lines), false for smooth rendering. Remarks When enabled, alternating scanlines are rendered at 3/4 brightness to simulate the appearance of a CRT monitor with visible scanlines. UseNonLumaContrastMask Gets or sets whether to use non-luma contrast masking for color fringing. public bool UseNonLumaContrastMask { get; set; } Property Value bool True to use alpha blending for color fringe reduction, false for full opacity. Remarks When enabled, pixels adjacent to color transitions are rendered with reduced alpha (transparency) to soften color artifacts and improve text readability. Uses graduated alpha values (0xff, 0xc0, 0x90, 0x70, 0x50) based on distance from lit pixels to create smooth color transitions. Methods ArrangeOverride(Size) Arranges the control within the final size allocated by parent layout. protected override Size ArrangeOverride(Size finalSize) Parameters finalSize Size Final size allocated to the control. Returns Size The final size (same as input). AttachFrameProvider(IFrameProvider) Attaches a frame provider to supply video frames for rendering. public void AttachFrameProvider(IFrameProvider provider) Parameters provider IFrameProvider Frame provider supplying rendered emulator output. Remarks Subscription Management: If a frame provider is already attached, unsubscribes from its events before subscribing to the new provider. Initial Frame: Retrieves the current frame immediately to ensure the display shows content even before the first frame event fires. AttachMachine(VA2M) Attaches the emulator machine for keyboard input injection. public void AttachMachine(VA2M machine) Parameters machine VA2M VA2M emulator instance to receive keyboard input. MeasureOverride(Size) Measures the desired size of the control. protected override Size MeasureOverride(Size availableSize) Parameters availableSize Size Available size from parent layout. Returns Size Desired size maintaining 563:384 aspect ratio. Remarks If available size is infinite, returns a baseline size of 700x525 pixels (maintaining approximately the correct aspect ratio). OnDetachedFromVisualTree(VisualTreeAttachmentEventArgs) Called when the control is detached from the visual tree. protected override void OnDetachedFromVisualTree(VisualTreeAttachmentEventArgs e) Parameters e VisualTreeAttachmentEventArgs Visual tree attachment event arguments. Remarks Unsubscribes from frame provider events to prevent memory leaks. Render(DrawingContext) Renders the control's visual content. public override void Render(DrawingContext context) Parameters context DrawingContext Drawing context for rendering operations. Remarks Rendering Pipeline: Ensure bitmap is allocated (563x384 WriteableBitmap) Lock bitmap for unsafe pointer access For each scanline (192 lines): Render line twice (double-scan to 384 lines) Choose monochrome or NTSC rendering based on mode and settings Apply scanline effect if enabled (3/4 brightness on odd lines) Draw scaled bitmap with aspect ratio preservation Rendering Mode Selection: Monochrome: ForceMono, non-graphics mode, or mixed text area with DefringeMixedText NTSC Color: Graphics mode without monochrome override RequestRefresh() Requests an immediate visual refresh of the display. public void RequestRefresh() Remarks Triggers InvalidateVisual() if a frame provider and frame data are available. Typically called by the UI refresh timer (60 Hz).

api/Pandowdy.UI.Apple2Font.html.title: Class Apple2Font | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.Apple2Font.html.summary: Class Apple2Font Namespace Pandowdy.UI Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Apple II Enhanced Video ROM font data. Contains the character bitmaps for the Apple II 40/80 column text modes. Source: a2e_enh_video.rom (4096 bytes, but using first 2048 bytes) The ROM file contains two font sets (4096 bytes total): First half (0-2047): 256 characters × 8 bytes = 2048 bytes (used) Second half (2048-4095): 256 characters × 8 bytes = 2048 bytes (reserved) public static class Apple2Font Inheritance object Apple2Font Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields FontData Apple II Enhanced Video ROM font data (2048 bytes). Each character occupies 8 bytes of bitmap data (7 pixels wide, 8 pixels tall). 256 characters × 8 bytes = 2048 bytes total. public static readonly byte[] FontData Field Value byte[] Methods GetCharacterBitmap(byte) Gets the font bitmap data for a specific character. public static byte[] GetCharacterBitmap(byte charCode) Parameters charCode byte ASCII character code (0-255) Returns byte[] 8-byte array containing the character bitmap (7 pixels wide, 8 pixels tall) Remarks Each byte represents one row of the character, with bits 0-6 representing the 7 pixels (bit 0 = leftmost pixel, bit 6 = rightmost pixel). Bit 7 is unused. GetCharacterRow(byte, int) Gets a single row of pixels for a character. public static byte GetCharacterRow(byte charCode, int row) Parameters charCode byte ASCII character code (0-255) row int Row index (0-7, where 0 is top row) Returns byte Byte representing 7 pixels (bits 0-6, where bit 0 is leftmost) Remarks This is more efficient than GetCharacterBitmap(byte) when only a single row is needed for rendering or analysis. Bit Layout: Bit 0 (LSB) = leftmost pixel, Bit 6 = rightmost pixel, Bit 7 = unused. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if row is not in range 0-7.

api/Pandowdy.UI.AvaloniaRefreshTicker.ht.title: Class AvaloniaRefreshTicker | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.AvaloniaRefreshTicker.ht.summary: Class AvaloniaRefreshTicker Namespace Pandowdy.UI Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Avalonia-based implementation of IRefreshTicker providing 60 Hz UI refresh timing. public sealed class AvaloniaRefreshTicker : IRefreshTicker Inheritance object AvaloniaRefreshTicker Implements IRefreshTicker Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides a 60 Hz timing signal for driving UI-based frame rendering and updates. Uses Avalonia's DispatcherTimer to ensure all timing events occur on the UI thread. Implementation: Uses Rx.NET's Subject pattern to create an observable stream of timestamps. The DispatcherTimer publishes to this stream at approximately 60 Hz (every ~16.67ms). Thread Safety: All timer callbacks execute on the Avalonia UI thread via DispatcherTimer, ensuring safe interaction with UI elements without explicit marshaling. Lifecycle: The timer starts when Start() is called and runs continuously until the application closes. The Stop() method is currently a no-op as Avalonia's DispatcherTimer automatically stops when the application shuts down. Usage Pattern: var ticker = new AvaloniaRefreshTicker(); ticker.Stream.Subscribe(timestamp => RefreshDisplay()); ticker.Start(); // Begin emitting 60 Hz updates Constructors AvaloniaRefreshTicker() Initializes a new instance of the AvaloniaRefreshTicker class. public AvaloniaRefreshTicker() Remarks Rx.NET Pattern: Creates a Subject that acts as both an observer (for publishing events) and an observable (for subscribers to receive events). Stream Setup: The Subject is wrapped in AsObservable() to prevent external code from directly calling OnNext/OnError/OnCompleted on the Subject. This encapsulation ensures only this class can publish events. No Timer Start: The constructor does not start the timer automatically. Call Start() to begin emitting timing events. Properties Stream Gets the observable stream that emits timestamps at approximately 60 Hz. public IObservable<DateTime> Stream { get; } Property Value IObservable<DateTime> An IObservable<T> that emits UTC timestamps each time the ticker fires. Remarks Frequency: Emits approximately 60 times per second (every ~16.67 milliseconds) when the ticker is running. Thread Context: All emissions occur on the Avalonia UI thread, making it safe to update UI elements directly from subscribers without additional marshaling. Timestamp Format: Provides DateTime.UtcNow at each emission. Subscribers can use these timestamps for time-based calculations or simply as periodic triggers. Subscription Example: ticker.Stream .Subscribe(timestamp => { // This executes on UI thread at ~60 Hz UpdateDisplay(); }); Methods Start() Starts the refresh ticker, causing it to begin emitting periodic timing signals at 60 Hz. public void Start() Remarks Timer Setup: Uses Avalonia's DispatcherTimer.Run() with a 16.67ms interval (1/60 second) to achieve approximately 60 Hz refresh rate. Callback Behavior: The timer callback returns true to indicate it should continue running. Each callback publishes the current UTC time to the stream via _subject.OnNext(DateTime.UtcNow). Idempotency: Calling Start() multiple times will create multiple timer instances. This is generally not recommended but won't cause crashes. Consider adding a guard flag if multiple Start() calls are a concern. Thread Affinity: All timer callbacks execute on the Avalonia UI dispatcher thread, ensuring safe UI interaction without explicit Invoke() calls. Timing Accuracy: The actual interval may vary slightly based on UI thread load and system scheduling, but should maintain an average close to 60 Hz for smooth animation. Stop() Stops the refresh ticker. Currently a no-op implementation. public void Stop() Remarks Current Implementation: This method is currently a no-op (does nothing). The DispatcherTimer will automatically stop when the Avalonia application closes. Future Enhancement: A proper implementation would: Store the timer handle returned by DispatcherTimer.Run() Call timer.Stop() or dispose the handle to halt timing events Potentially call _observer.OnCompleted() to signal stream completion Current Behavior: Timers continue running until application shutdown. For most use cases (single ticker for application lifetime), this is acceptable. Memory Implications: If creating/disposing multiple tickers during application lifetime, the lack of Stop() implementation could result in multiple timer instances running simultaneously.

api/Pandowdy.UI.Helpers.WindowSettingsHe.title: Class WindowSettingsHelper | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.Helpers.WindowSettingsHe.summary: Class WindowSettingsHelper Namespace Pandowdy.UI.Helpers Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Utility class for saving and restoring window position and size settings. public static class WindowSettingsHelper Inheritance object WindowSettingsHelper Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Windows 11 Compatibility: Implements workarounds for Windows 11's aggressive window placement algorithm that overrides saved positions. Multi-Monitor Support: Tracks which monitor the window was on and validates that saved positions are still on-screen when monitors are reconfigured. DPI Awareness: Stores positions in pixel coordinates which Avalonia automatically adjusts for DPI scaling. Methods Load() Loads window settings from the application's configuration directory. public static WindowSettings? Load() Returns WindowSettings WindowSettings if successfully loaded and validated, null if file doesn't exist or settings are invalid. Remarks Performs validation to ensure loaded settings represent a valid on-screen position. Returns null if settings file is missing, corrupted, or contains invalid positions. Restore(Window, WindowSettings?) Restores window position and size from saved settings. public static void Restore(Window window, WindowSettings? settings) Parameters window Window Window to restore settings to. settings WindowSettings Previously saved settings, or null to use defaults. Remarks Validation: Checks that the saved position is still on a visible screen. If the monitor was disconnected or position is off-screen, uses default centered position. Windows 11 Note: This method should be called BEFORE window.Show() for best results. Windows 11 is less likely to override positions set before the window becomes visible. Save(Window) Saves window settings to the application's configuration directory. public static void Save(Window window) Parameters window Window Window to save settings from. Remarks Captures current position, size, maximized state, and monitor information. Important: If the window is maximized, this saves the "restore bounds" (the size the window will be when un-maximized), not the maximized size. This ensures that when the window is restored next time, it has a reasonable normal size even if it was closed while maximized. Creates configuration directory if it doesn't exist. Silently ignores save errors to prevent application crashes.

api/Pandowdy.UI.Interfaces.IMainWindowFa.summary: Interface IMainWindowFactory Namespace Pandowdy.UI.Interfaces Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Factory for creating and initializing MainWindow instances with proper dependency injection. Encapsulates the two-phase initialization required by Avalonia's parameterless constructor constraint. public interface IMainWindowFactory Methods Create() Creates a new MainWindow instance and initializes it with all required dependencies. This method handles both construction and initialization atomically. MainWindow Create() Returns MainWindow A fully initialized MainWindow ready to be displayed.

api/Pandowdy.UI.Interfaces.IRefreshTicke.title: Interface IRefreshTicker | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.Interfaces.IRefreshTicke.summary: Interface IRefreshTicker Namespace Pandowdy.UI.Interfaces Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Provides a 60 Hz refresh signal for driving UI-based frame rendering and updates. public interface IRefreshTicker Remarks This interface represents a timing source that emits periodic signals at approximately 60 Hz (60 times per second), matching the typical video refresh rate of the Apple IIe and modern displays. It's designed to be implemented by UI-layer components (such as Avalonia's DispatcherTimer) to drive frame rendering in a way that's synchronized with the UI thread and display refresh. The ticker uses the Observable pattern (Rx.NET) to provide a reactive stream of timestamps. Subscribers can use this stream to trigger frame generation, display updates, and other time-based operations that need to occur at regular intervals. Implementation Note: This interface is implemented in the UI layer using platform-specific timing mechanisms (DispatcherTimer, animation frames, etc.) that are only available in the UI context. Properties Stream Gets an observable stream that emits timestamps at approximately 60 Hz. IObservable<DateTime> Stream { get; } Property Value IObservable<DateTime> An IObservable<T> that emits the current timestamp each time the ticker fires. Subscribers can observe this stream to receive periodic notifications for driving frame updates. Remarks The stream emits approximately 60 times per second when the ticker is running. Each emission provides the current DateTime at the time of the tick, which can be used for time-based calculations or simply as a signal to perform periodic work. Typical usage pattern: ticker.Stream .ObserveOn(RxApp.MainThreadScheduler) .Subscribe(_ => RenderFrame()); The actual timing may vary slightly based on system load and UI thread scheduling, but should maintain an average rate close to 60 Hz for smooth animation. Methods Start() Starts the refresh ticker, causing it to begin emitting periodic signals. void Start() Remarks After calling this method, the Stream will begin emitting timestamps at approximately 60 Hz. This method is typically called when the emulator starts running or when the UI becomes visible and ready to display frames. Calling Start() on an already-running ticker should be safe (idempotent). Stop() Stops the refresh ticker, causing it to cease emitting signals. void Stop() Remarks After calling this method, the Stream will stop emitting timestamps until Start() is called again. This method is typically called when the emulator is paused or when the UI is no longer visible. Calling Stop() on an already-stopped ticker should be safe (idempotent).

object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks ⚠️ Construction: Do not construct this class directly. Use MainWindowFactory to create properly initialized instances with all dependencies injected. Architecture: This class serves as the "View" in the MVVM pattern, handling UI-specific concerns like window lifecycle, keyboard focus management, and bridging between the view model and emulator core components. Dependency Injection Constraint: Avalonia requires windows to have a parameterless constructor for XAML loading. To work around this limitation while maintaining testability and avoiding service locator anti-pattern, this class uses a two-phase initialization: Constructor: Parameterless, called by XAML loader, minimal initialization Initialize(): Called immediately after construction by MainWindowFactory, injects dependencies Both phases are handled automatically by Create(). Key Responsibilities: Window lifecycle management (OnOpened, OnClosed) Emulator thread management (start/stop/reset) Keyboard input routing and accelerator handling Settings persistence (save/restore window size and preferences) Focus management (keeping keyboard focus on display) Bridging view model commands to emulator actions Thread Coordination: Manages cross-thread communication between: UI thread (Avalonia dispatcher) Emulator thread (Task.Run background thread) Refresh ticker (60 Hz UI updates) Usage Example: // Correct: Use the factory var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>(); var mainWindow = factory.Create(); mainWindow.Show(); // Incorrect: Manual construction var mainWindow = new MainWindow(); // Missing dependencies! Constructors MainWindow() Initializes a new instance of the MainWindow class. public MainWindow() Remarks ⚠️ Important: Do not construct MainWindow directly. Use MainWindowFactory instead, which properly handles two-phase initialization and dependency injection. XAML Requirement: This parameterless constructor is required by Avalonia's XAML loader. Heavy initialization and dependency injection are deferred to the Initialize(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker) method. Setup: Loads XAML via InitializeComponent() Registers menu pointer event handlers for focus management Does NOT attach machine or frame provider (deferred to Initialize) Call Sequence: This constructor is called first by the XAML loader or MainWindowFactory, immediately followed by Initialize(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker) (managed by MainWindowFactory). Recommended Pattern: // Correct: Use factory var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>(); var window = factory.Create(); // Incorrect: Manual construction (missing dependencies!) var window = new MainWindow(); Properties IsCapsLockEnabledForInput Gets whether caps lock emulation is currently enabled. public bool IsCapsLockEnabledForInput { get; } Property Value bool True to convert lowercase to uppercase, false to preserve case. Remarks Exposed to Apple2Display control for keyboard input processing. The Apple IIe keyboard was uppercase-only, so this defaults to true for authenticity. Methods Initialize(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker) Initializes the MainWindow with all required dependencies. Must be called immediately after construction (handled by MainWindowFactory). public void Initialize(MainWindowViewModel viewModel, VA2M machine, IFrameProvider frameProvider, IRefreshTicker refreshTicker) Parameters viewModel MainWindowViewModel Main window view model containing UI state and commands. machine VA2M Apple IIe emulator machine instance. frameProvider IFrameProvider Provider supplying rendered video frames. refreshTicker IRefreshTicker 60 Hz ticker for driving display updates. Remarks ⚠️ Important: Do not call this method directly. Use Create() which handles the complete construction and initialization sequence correctly. Two-Phase Initialization: This method completes the initialization started by the parameterless constructor, injecting all dependencies that couldn't be provided through constructor parameters due to Avalonia's XAML requirements. Why Two-Phase? Avalonia requires windows to have parameterless constructors for XAML compilation. Since we can't inject dependencies via constructor, we use this separate Initialize() method called by MainWindowFactory immediately after construction. Initialization Steps: Validate single initialization (throw if called twice) Set ViewModel and DataContext Store machine and refresh ticker references Attach machine and frame provider to Apple2Display control Initialize SoftSwitchStatusPanel with its view model Set up ReactiveUI subscriptions for view model property changes Bridge command executions to emulator actions Restore settings from configuration file Reactive Subscriptions: Subscribes to view model properties and propagates changes to emulator and display: ThrottleEnabled: Controls CPU speed (1.023 MHz vs unthrottled) CapsLockEnabled: Controls keyboard uppercase conversion ShowScanLines: Controls CRT scanline effect ForceMonochrome: Controls color vs monochrome display DecreaseContrast: Controls contrast reduction MonoMixed: Controls mixed mode text defring Command Bridging: Links view model commands to code-behind event handlers: StartEmu → OnEmuStartClicked StopEmu → OnEmuStopClicked ResetEmu → OnEmuResetClicked StepOnce → OnEmuStepOnceClicked Recommended Pattern: // Let the factory handle everything: var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>(); var window = factory.Create(); // Calls both new MainWindow() and Initialize() Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if Initialize is called more than once, or if ScreenDisplay control is not found in XAML. InitializeComponent(bool, bool) Wires up the controls and optionally loads XAML markup and attaches dev tools (if Avalonia.Diagnostics package is referenced). [ExcludeFromCodeCoverage] public void InitializeComponent(bool loadXaml = true, bool attachDevTools = true) Parameters loadXaml bool Should the XAML be loaded into the component. attachDevTools bool Should the dev tools be attached. OnClosed(EventArgs) Called when the window is closed (after disposal). protected override void OnClosed(EventArgs e) Parameters e EventArgs Event arguments. Remarks Cleanup Sequence: Dispose refresh ticker subscription Stop refresh ticker Stop emulator thread (cancel token) Call base.OnClosed() Note: Settings are saved in OnClosing, not here, because by this point the window's platform implementation has been disposed and we can't access window properties. Clean Shutdown: Ensures all background threads and subscriptions are properly disposed to prevent resource leaks and allow graceful application exit. OnClosing(WindowClosingEventArgs) Called when the window is closing (before disposal). protected override void OnClosing(WindowClosingEventArgs e) Parameters e WindowClosingEventArgs Cancel event arguments (can cancel close). Remarks Shutdown Sequence: Save window position/size to configuration file Save display settings to configuration file Important: Settings are saved in OnClosing (not OnClosed) because by the time OnClosed fires, the window's platform implementation has been disposed and we can't access Position, Width, Height, or Screens Properties. Settings Persistence: Saves both window geometry (position/size) and display settings (scanlines, colors, etc.) to separate configuration files for cleaner organization and independent loading. Uses tracked "normal" bounds if window is maximized to ensure proper restoration. OnGotFocus(GotFocusEventArgs) Called when the window receives keyboard focus. protected override void OnGotFocus(GotFocusEventArgs e) Parameters e GotFocusEventArgs Focus event arguments. Remarks If the pointer is not over the menu bar, redirects focus to the Apple2Display control to ensure keyboard input is captured for emulator input. OnKeyDown(KeyEventArgs) Handles key down events for Apple IIe pushbutton emulation. protected override void OnKeyDown(KeyEventArgs e) Parameters e KeyEventArgs Key event arguments. Remarks Currently only handles Shift key for pushbutton 2. Does NOT mark event as handled so shift combinations (Shift+F5, etc.) still work. F1 and F2 pushbutton presses are handled in HandleAccelerator() instead. OnKeyUp(KeyEventArgs) Handles key up events for Apple IIe pushbutton emulation. protected override void OnKeyUp(KeyEventArgs e) Parameters e KeyEventArgs Key event arguments. Remarks Pushbutton Mapping: F1: Pushbutton 0 (game controller button) F2: Pushbutton 1 (game controller button) Shift (Left/Right): Pushbutton 2 (open-apple/closed-apple) Releases the corresponding pushbutton when the key is released. Marks event as handled to prevent further processing. OnOpened(EventArgs) Called when the window is opened and visible. protected override void OnOpened(EventArgs e) Parameters e EventArgs Event arguments. Remarks Startup Sequence: Apply Windows 11 position fallback (reapply position after 100ms delay) Set keyboard focus to Apple2Display control Start the 60 Hz refresh ticker Subscribe to refresh stream and trigger display updates Post command to start emulator (begins emulation automatically) Windows 11 Workaround: Even though we set position before Show() in MainWindowFactory, Windows 11 sometimes overrides it. This fallback reapplies the position 100ms after the window opens, which usually succeeds where the initial attempt failed. Refresh Ticker: Subscribes to 60 Hz timing signal and calls screenDisplay.RequestRefresh() on each tick. All updates are marshaled to the UI thread via RxApp.MainThreadScheduler. Auto-Start: Uses Dispatcher.UIThread.Post to start the emulator after the window is fully opened, ensuring smooth startup without blocking the UI thread. OnPointerPressed(PointerPressedEventArgs) Called when the window receives a pointer (mouse) press event. protected override void OnPointerPressed(PointerPressedEventArgs e) Parameters e PointerPressedEventArgs Pointer pressed event arguments. Remarks If the pointer is not over the menu bar, redirects focus to the Apple2Display control to ensure keyboard input is captured for emulator input after mouse clicks.

api/Pandowdy.UI.MainWindowFactory.html.title: Class MainWindowFactory | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.MainWindowFactory.html.summary: Class MainWindowFactory Namespace Pandowdy.UI Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Factory for creating fully-initialized MainWindow instances with proper dependency injection. public sealed class MainWindowFactory : IMainWindowFactory Inheritance object MainWindowFactory Implements IMainWindowFactory Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides a clean abstraction over MainWindow's two-phase initialization pattern, encapsulating the complexity of constructor + Initialize() calls into a single Create() method. Why This Factory Exists: Avalonia requires windows to have parameterless constructors for XAML compilation, preventing direct constructor-based dependency injection. This factory bridges that gap by: Accepting all dependencies via its own constructor (enabling DI container registration) Creating MainWindow with parameterless constructor (satisfying Avalonia) Calling Initialize() with all dependencies (completing setup) Returning a fully functional window ready for Show() Dependency Injection Pattern: This factory is registered as a singleton in the DI container and receives all MainWindow dependencies. When Create() is called, it transfers those dependencies to the new MainWindow instance. Thread Safety: This factory is NOT thread-safe for concurrent Create() calls, but this is acceptable because Create() is only called once during application startup from the UI thread. Usage Example: // In DI container registration (Program.cs or similar): services.AddSingleton<IMainWindowFactory, MainWindowFactory>(); // In application startup: var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>(); var mainWindow = factory.Create(); mainWindow.Show(); Constructors MainWindowFactory(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker) Factory for creating fully-initialized MainWindow instances with proper dependency injection. public MainWindowFactory(MainWindowViewModel viewModel, VA2M machine, IFrameProvider frameProvider, IRefreshTicker refreshTicker) Parameters viewModel MainWindowViewModel The main window view model containing UI state and commands. Must not be null. machine VA2M The Apple IIe emulator instance (VA2M) that executes 6502 instructions and manages system state. Must not be null. frameProvider IFrameProvider The frame provider supplying rendered video frames (560x192 pixels) from the emulator to the display control. Must not be null. refreshTicker IRefreshTicker The 60 Hz refresh ticker that triggers periodic display updates, ensuring smooth animation and responsive UI. Must not be null. Remarks Purpose: Provides a clean abstraction over MainWindow's two-phase initialization pattern, encapsulating the complexity of constructor + Initialize() calls into a single Create() method. Why This Factory Exists: Avalonia requires windows to have parameterless constructors for XAML compilation, preventing direct constructor-based dependency injection. This factory bridges that gap by: Accepting all dependencies via its own constructor (enabling DI container registration) Creating MainWindow with parameterless constructor (satisfying Avalonia) Calling Initialize() with all dependencies (completing setup) Returning a fully functional window ready for Show() Dependency Injection Pattern: This factory is registered as a singleton in the DI container and receives all MainWindow dependencies. When Create() is called, it transfers those dependencies to the new MainWindow instance. Thread Safety: This factory is NOT thread-safe for concurrent Create() calls, but this is acceptable because Create() is only called once during application startup from the UI thread. Usage Example: // In DI container registration (Program.cs or similar): services.AddSingleton<IMainWindowFactory, MainWindowFactory>(); // In application startup: var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>(); var mainWindow = factory.Create(); mainWindow.Show(); Methods Create() Creates a new MainWindow instance and initializes it with all dependencies. public MainWindow Create() Returns MainWindow A fully initialized MainWindow ready to be shown. All dependencies are injected, reactive subscriptions are set up, position/size restored, and settings loaded. Remarks Atomic Operation: This method performs both construction and initialization as a single atomic operation, ensuring the returned window is always in a consistent, fully-initialized state. Creation Steps: Call MainWindow parameterless constructor (XAML loading, minimal setup) Call window.Initialize() with all dependencies (complete setup) Restore window position/size BEFORE showing (Windows 11 compatibility) Return fully-initialized window Windows 11 Position Restore: Window position and size are restored BEFORE the window is shown. This significantly improves the chance that Windows 11 will respect the saved position instead of applying its own "smart" placement algorithm. Multi-Monitor Support: Validates that saved positions are still on-screen. If the monitor was disconnected or position is invalid, falls back to centered on primary. What's Initialized: ViewModel and DataContext set Machine and frame provider attached to Apple2Display SoftSwitchStatusPanel initialized with its view model ReactiveUI subscriptions set up (7 property subscriptions + 4 command bridges) Window position/size restored from saved settings Display settings restored from configuration file Exception Safety: If Initialize() throws an exception (e.g., ScreenDisplay control not found in XAML), the exception propagates to the caller. The partially-constructed window should be discarded. Usage: var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>(); var window = factory.Create(); window.Show(); // Window is ready to show at saved position Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown by MainWindow.Initialize() if called more than once (shouldn't happen in normal usage) or if required controls (e.g., ScreenDisplay) are not found in XAML.
