Exception) AvaloniaObject.RaisePropertyChanged<T>(DirectPropertyBase<T>, T, T) AvaloniaObject.SetAndRaise<T>(DirectPropertyBase<T>, ref T, T) AvaloniaObject.InheritanceParent AvaloniaObject.this[AvaloniaProperty] AvaloniaObject.this[IndexerDescriptor] AvaloniaObject.PropertyChanged object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides a visual panel showing the current state of all Apple IIe soft switches, including memory mapping configuration, video modes, pushbuttons, and annunciators. This is invaluable for debugging, education, and understanding what the emulated Apple IIe is doing. Display Categories: Memory Mapping: 80STORE, RAMRD, RAMWRT, INTCXROM, ALTZP, SLOTC3ROM Video Modes: PAGE2, HIRES, MIXED, TEXT, 80COL, ALTCHAR, FLASHON Pushbuttons: PB0, PB1, PB2 (game controller buttons) Annunciators: AN0, AN1, AN2, AN3/DGR Language Card: BANK1, PREWRITE, HIGHREAD, HIGHWRITE Two-Phase Initialization: Similar to MainWindow, this control uses a two-phase initialization pattern: Constructor: Parameterless (Avalonia requirement), loads XAML Initialize(): Called by parent window, sets DataContext to view model Data Binding: All status displays are bound to properties in SystemStatusViewModel, which receives updates from the emulator core via ISystemStatusProvider. Update Frequency: Updates occur whenever soft switch states change in the emulator. Unlike EmulatorStateViewModel (which samples at 50ms), these updates are event-driven and happen immediately when switches change. Visibility Control: The panel can be shown/hidden via MainWindow.ShowSoftSwitchStatus property, allowing users to toggle it on/off (Alt+W shortcut). Constructors SoftSwitchStatusPanel() Initializes a new instance of the SoftSwitchStatusPanel class. public SoftSwitchStatusPanel() Remarks XAML Requirement: This parameterless constructor is required by Avalonia's XAML loader. The DataContext is not set here; it must be set via Initialize(SystemStatusViewModel) Initialization: Only loads the XAML markup. The actual view model binding is deferred to the Initialize() method which is called by the parent MainWindow. Call Sequence: This constructor is called first (by XAML loader or parent), immediately followed by Initialize() (called by MainWindow.Initialize). Methods Initialize(SystemStatusViewModel) Initializes the panel with its view model and establishes data binding. Must be called by parent window after construction. public void Initialize(SystemStatusViewModel viewModel) Parameters viewModel SystemStatusViewModel The SystemStatusViewModel containing reactive properties for all 24 soft switch states. Must not be null. Remarks Two-Phase Pattern: This method completes the initialization started by the parameterless constructor, setting the DataContext to enable XAML data binding. Data Binding Setup: Once DataContext is set to the SystemStatusViewModel, all XAML bindings become active and the panel starts displaying real-time soft switch states. Single Initialization: The _isInitialized guard prevents multiple Initialize() calls, which would be an error since DataContext should only be set once. Called By: MainWindow.Initialize() during the window's two-phase initialization process. This happens automatically after window construction. View Model Properties: The SystemStatusViewModel contains 24 reactive boolean properties representing all Apple IIe soft switches. When any switch changes in the emulator, the view model property updates, triggering UI refresh via data binding. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if Initialize is called more than once. InitializeComponent(bool) Wires up the controls and optionally loads XAML markup and attaches dev tools (if Avalonia.Diagnostics package is referenced). [ExcludeFromCodeCoverage] public void InitializeComponent(bool loadXaml = true) Parameters loadXaml bool Should the XAML be loaded into the component.

api/Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels.EmulatorState.title: Class EmulatorStateViewModel | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels.EmulatorState.summary: Class EmulatorStateViewModel Namespace Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll View model for displaying real-time Apple IIe emulator state (PC, cycles, BASIC line number). public sealed class EmulatorStateViewModel : ReactiveObject, IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>, IHandleObservableErrors, IReactiveObject, INotifyPropertyChanged, INotifyPropertyChanging, IEnableLogger, IActivatableViewModel Inheritance object ReactiveObject EmulatorStateViewModel Implements IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject> IHandleObservableErrors IReactiveObject INotifyPropertyChanged INotifyPropertyChanging IEnableLogger IActivatableViewModel Inherited Members ReactiveObject.SuppressChangeNotifications() ReactiveObject.AreChangeNotificationsEnabled() ReactiveObject.DelayChangeNotifications() ReactiveObject.Changing ReactiveObject.Changed ReactiveObject.ThrownExceptions ReactiveObject.PropertyChanging ReactiveObject.PropertyChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides reactive properties bound to UI elements that display the current execution state of the emulator. Updates are automatically pushed from the emulator core via the IEmulatorState stream. Reactive Pattern: Implements ReactiveUI's IActivatableViewModel pattern for lifecycle-aware subscriptions. The view model only subscribes to the state stream when activated (view is visible), preventing unnecessary updates and resource usage. Update Frequency: Samples the emulator state stream at 50ms intervals (20 Hz) to balance UI responsiveness with performance. This prevents excessive UI updates while still providing smooth feedback during emulation. Thread Safety: All property updates are marshaled to the UI thread via RxApp.MainThreadScheduler, ensuring safe binding to UI elements. Typical Usage: <TextBlock Text="{Binding PC, StringFormat='PC: ${0:X4}'}" /> <TextBlock Text="{Binding Cycles, StringFormat='Cycles: {0:N0}'}" /> <TextBlock Text="{Binding LineNumber, StringFormat='Line: {0}'}" /> Constructors EmulatorStateViewModel(IEmulatorState) Initializes a new instance of the EmulatorStateViewModel class. public EmulatorStateViewModel(IEmulatorState state) Parameters state IEmulatorState Emulator state provider supplying reactive state updates. Remarks Initialization: The constructor sets up a reactive subscription using ReactiveUI's WhenActivated pattern. The actual subscription to the state stream is deferred until the view model is activated (typically when the view becomes visible). Subscription Lifecycle: Activation: When view becomes visible, WhenActivated callback fires Subscription: Subscribe to state stream with 50ms sampling Update Properties: PC, LineNumber, Cycles updated on UI thread Deactivation: When view is hidden/disposed, subscription is automatically disposed Sampling Strategy: Uses Observable.Sample(50ms) to throttle updates to 20 Hz. This prevents excessive UI updates (emulator can generate updates at much higher frequency) while maintaining smooth visual feedback. Thread Marshaling: ObserveOn(RxApp.MainThreadScheduler) ensures all property updates occur on the UI thread, which is required for binding to UI elements. Memory Management: The WhenActivated pattern automatically handles subscription disposal, preventing memory leaks when the view model is no longer active. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if state is null. Properties Activator Gets the view model activator for managing subscription lifecycle. public ViewModelActivator Activator { get; } Property Value ViewModelActivator Remarks Used by ReactiveUI's WhenActivated mechanism to automatically subscribe when the view model is active (view visible) and unsubscribe when inactive (view hidden/disposed). This prevents memory leaks and unnecessary processing. Cycles Gets the total number of CPU cycles executed since the last reset. public ulong Cycles { get; } Property Value ulong Cycle count (0 to ulong.MaxValue), increments at 1.023 MHz rate. Remarks Cycle Timing: The Apple IIe runs at approximately 1,023,000 Hz (1.023 MHz). This property shows the total number of cycles executed, which can be used to: Calculate elapsed time: cycles / 1,023,000 = seconds Measure performance of BASIC or machine code programs Verify emulator timing accuracy Display uptime or execution duration Example Values: 1,023,000 cycles ≈ 1 second of emulation 17,063 cycles ≈ 1 video frame (~60 Hz) 61,380,000 cycles ≈ 1 minute of emulation Reset Behavior: Reset to 0 on full system reset (power cycle). Continues counting during warm reset (Ctrl+Reset). LineNumber Gets the current Applesoft BASIC line number being executed, or null if not in BASIC. public int? LineNumber { get; } Property Value int? Line number (0-63999) if executing BASIC code, null if in Monitor or machine code. Remarks Detection Method: The emulator reads zero page locations $75-$76 which contain the BASIC line pointer. If the value is less than $FA00, it's interpreted as a valid BASIC line number. Use Cases: Display current line during BASIC program execution Help users debug BASIC programs by showing execution progress Null when in Monitor, running machine code, or in immediate mode Binding Example: <TextBlock Text="{Binding LineNumber, StringFormat='Line: {0}', TargetNullValue='(not in BASIC)'}" /> PC Gets the current 6502 Program Counter (PC) register value. public ushort PC { get; } Property Value ushort 16-bit address ($0000-$FFFF) indicating the next instruction to execute. Remarks The PC points to the memory address of the next instruction the CPU will execute. In the Apple IIe, common PC values include: $F666: Monitor prompt $E000-$FFFF: ROM (Monitor, BASIC interpreter) $0800-$BFFF: User program space Updates approximately 20 times per second (50ms sample interval) when the view model is active.

api/Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels.MainWindowVie.title: Class MainWindowViewModel | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels.MainWindowVie.summary: Class MainWindowViewModel Namespace Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll Main view model for the application window, coordinating child view models and UI state. public sealed class MainWindowViewModel : ReactiveObject, IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>, IHandleObservableErrors, IReactiveObject, INotifyPropertyChanged, INotifyPropertyChanging, IEnableLogger Inheritance object ReactiveObject MainWindowViewModel Implements IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject> IHandleObservableErrors IReactiveObject INotifyPropertyChanged INotifyPropertyChanging IEnableLogger Inherited Members ReactiveObject.SuppressChangeNotifications() ReactiveObject.AreChangeNotificationsEnabled() ReactiveObject.DelayChangeNotifications() ReactiveObject.Changing ReactiveObject.Changed ReactiveObject.ThrownExceptions ReactiveObject.PropertyChanging ReactiveObject.PropertyChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Architecture: This view model serves as the root composition point for all child view models and coordinates application-level state and commands. It follows the MVVM pattern with ReactiveUI for property change notifications and command execution. Child View Models: EmulatorState: Displays CPU state (PC, cycles, BASIC line) SystemStatus: Displays soft switches and system status Responsibility Separation: This view model manages UI-level state (menu toggles, display options) but delegates emulator control to IEmulatorState. The view (MainWindow.axaml.cs) bridges between these commands and actual emulator services. Command Pattern: Uses ReactiveCommand for all user actions, enabling easy binding to menu items and keyboard shortcuts while maintaining testability. Constructors MainWindowViewModel(EmulatorStateViewModel, IEmulatorState, SystemStatusViewModel) Initializes a new instance of the MainWindowViewModel class. public MainWindowViewModel(EmulatorStateViewModel emulatorState, IEmulatorState emuState, SystemStatusViewModel systemStatus) Parameters emulatorState EmulatorStateViewModel View model for displaying CPU state. emuState IEmulatorState Emulator state provider for control commands. systemStatus SystemStatusViewModel View model for displaying system status. Remarks Dependency Injection: All dependencies are injected via constructor, enabling testability and loose coupling. Child view models are created and managed by the DI container. Command Initialization: All ReactiveCommands are initialized in the constructor. Commands that modify properties use inline lambdas for simplicity. Future View Models: ErrorLog and Disassembly view models are commented out pending implementation of those features. Placeholder Commands: StartEmu, StopEmu, ResetEmu, and StepOnce are currently empty commands. The view (MainWindow.axaml.cs) handles these actions directly. Future refactoring may move this logic into the view model for better separation of concerns and testability. Properties CapsLockEnabled Gets or sets whether Caps Lock emulation is enabled. public bool CapsLockEnabled { get; set; } Property Value bool True to enable Caps Lock behavior, false to disable. Remarks When enabled, keyboard input is converted to uppercase (matching Apple IIe behavior). When disabled, keyboard input preserves case. The Apple IIe keyboard was uppercase-only (no lowercase letters in ROM character set). This option allows modern users to type naturally while maintaining authentic behavior. ContinueCommand Gets the command to continue emulator execution after pause. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ContinueCommand { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that requests the emulator to resume running. Remarks When executed, calls IEmulatorState.RequestContinue() which signals the emulator to resume continuous execution (restart RunAsync loop). DecreaseContrast Gets or sets whether to decrease display contrast for a softer appearance. public bool DecreaseContrast { get; set; } Property Value bool True to decrease contrast, false for normal contrast. Remarks Reduces the intensity difference between bright and dark pixels, simulating an aged CRT monitor or reducing eye strain. EmulatorState Gets the view model for displaying emulator CPU state. public EmulatorStateViewModel EmulatorState { get; } Property Value EmulatorStateViewModel View model showing PC, cycles, and BASIC line number. ForceMonochrome Gets or sets whether to force monochrome (green or amber) display mode. public bool ForceMonochrome { get; set; } Property Value bool True to force monochrome, false for color display. Remarks When enabled, renders output in monochrome (simulating a green or amber phosphor monitor). When disabled, uses NTSC color artifact emulation. Many early Apple IIe users had monochrome monitors which were sharper and easier to read for text. This option replicates that experience. MonoMixed Gets or sets whether to use monochrome rendering in mixed text/graphics mode. public bool MonoMixed { get; set; } Property Value bool True for monochrome mixed mode, false for color. Remarks Mixed mode displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom. This option controls whether the graphics portion is rendered in monochrome or color. Some programs look better in monochrome mixed mode, particularly those designed for monochrome monitors. PauseCommand Gets the command to pause emulator execution. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> PauseCommand { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that requests the emulator to pause (RunAsync loop exits). Remarks When executed, calls IEmulatorState.RequestPause() which signals the emulator to stop continuous execution. The emulator remains initialized and can be resumed with ContinueCommand. ResetEmu Gets the command to reset the emulator (full system reset / power cycle). public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ResetEmu { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that performs a cold boot reset. Remarks Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual reset logic is handled by the view which calls VA2M.Reset() directly. Future enhancement may move reset control into the view model for better testability. ShowScanLines Gets or sets whether CRT scanline effect is shown on the display. public bool ShowScanLines { get; set; } Property Value bool True to show scanlines, false for smooth display. Remarks When enabled, adds alternating dark lines to simulate the appearance of a CRT monitor. When disabled, shows a clean, modern display. This is a cosmetic option for nostalgia/authenticity. Does not affect emulation accuracy. ShowSoftSwitchStatus Gets or sets whether the soft switch status panel is visible. public bool ShowSoftSwitchStatus { get; set; } Property Value bool True to show the status panel, false to hide it. Remarks Controls the visibility of the right-side panel displaying Apple IIe soft switch states (memory mapping, video modes, ROM selection, annunciators, pushbuttons). Users can toggle this to maximize screen space for the display or show detailed system status for debugging and learning. StartEmu Gets the command to start emulator execution. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StartEmu { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that initiates continuous emulation. Remarks Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual start logic is handled by the view (MainWindow.axaml.cs) which calls VA2M.RunAsync() directly when the window is loaded. Future enhancement may move start/stop control into the view model for better testability. StepCommand Gets the command to execute a single CPU instruction (step mode). public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StepCommand { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that executes one instruction while paused. Remarks When executed, calls IEmulatorState.RequestStep() which runs exactly one CPU clock cycle. Useful for debugging and single-stepping through code. StepOnce Gets the command to execute a single CPU instruction. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StepOnce { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that steps one instruction while paused. Remarks Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual step logic is handled by StepCommand which calls IEmulatorState.RequestStep(). This may be redundant with StepCommand and could be removed or consolidated. StopEmu Gets the command to stop emulator execution. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StopEmu { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that halts continuous emulation. Remarks Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual stop logic is handled by the view which cancels the RunAsync() task. Future enhancement may move start/stop control into the view model for better testability. SystemStatus Gets the view model for displaying system status (soft switches, buttons). public SystemStatusViewModel SystemStatus { get; } Property Value SystemStatusViewModel View model showing soft switch states and pushbutton status. ThrottleEnabled Gets or sets whether CPU throttling is enabled to maintain 1.023 MHz speed. public bool ThrottleEnabled { get; set; } Property Value bool True to run at Apple IIe speed, false to run as fast as possible. Remarks When true, the emulator runs at approximately 1.023 MHz (authentic Apple IIe speed). When false, runs unthrottled (useful for loading programs quickly or testing). This property is bound to a menu item checkbox. Changes are observed by the view which propagates them to the VA2M.ThrottleEnabled property. ToggleCapsLock Gets the command to toggle Caps Lock emulation on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleCapsLock { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the CapsLockEnabled property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates CapsLockEnabled which the view observes to control keyboard input transformation. ToggleDecreaseContrast Gets the command to toggle reduced contrast display on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleDecreaseContrast { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the DecreaseContrast property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates DecreaseContrast which the view observes to control display intensity. ToggleMonoMixed Gets the command to toggle monochrome mixed mode on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleMonoMixed { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the MonoMixed property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates MonoMixed which the view observes to control mixed mode color rendering. ToggleMonochrome Gets the command to toggle monochrome display mode on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleMonochrome { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the ForceMonochrome property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ForceMonochrome which the view observes to control color vs monochrome rendering. ToggleScanLines Gets the command to toggle scanline display effect on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleScanLines { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the ShowScanLines property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ShowScanLines which the view observes to control display rendering. ToggleSoftSwitchStatus Gets the command to toggle soft switch status panel visibility on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleSoftSwitchStatus { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the ShowSoftSwitchStatus property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ShowSoftSwitchStatus which the view observes to control status panel visibility. ToggleThrottle Gets the command to toggle CPU throttling on/off. public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleThrottle { get; } Property Value ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> Command that inverts the ThrottleEnabled property. Remarks Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ThrottleEnabled which the view observes to update VA2M.ThrottleEnabled.

api/Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels.SystemStatusV.title: Class SystemStatusViewModel | Pandowdy Documentation
api/Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels.SystemStatusV.summary: Class SystemStatusViewModel Namespace Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels Assembly Pandowdy.UI.dll View model for displaying Apple IIe system status including soft switches and pushbutton states. public sealed class SystemStatusViewModel : ReactiveObject, IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>, IHandleObservableErrors, IReactiveObject, INotifyPropertyChanged, INotifyPropertyChanging, IEnableLogger Inheritance object ReactiveObject SystemStatusViewModel Implements IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject> IHandleObservableErrors IReactiveObject INotifyPropertyChanged INotifyPropertyChanging IEnableLogger Inherited Members ReactiveObject.SuppressChangeNotifications() ReactiveObject.AreChangeNotificationsEnabled() ReactiveObject.DelayChangeNotifications() ReactiveObject.Changing ReactiveObject.Changed ReactiveObject.ThrownExceptions ReactiveObject.PropertyChanging ReactiveObject.PropertyChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Purpose: Provides reactive properties for all Apple IIe soft switches, pushbuttons, and system state flags. These properties are bound to UI elements that display the current hardware configuration and status. Update Pattern: Unlike EmulatorStateViewModel, this view model uses immediate subscription (no WhenActivated) because system status updates are relatively infrequent and always needed for accurate display. Updates are pushed from ISystemStatusProvider.Stream. Initialization: The constructor immediately subscribes to the status stream and applies the current snapshot to initialize all properties with correct initial values. Property Count: Contains 24 boolean properties representing: 6 memory mapping switches (80STORE, RAMRD, RAMWRT, INTCXROM, ALTZP, SLOTC3ROM) 3 pushbuttons (PB0, PB1, PB2) 4 annunciators (AN0, AN1, AN2, AN3/DGR) 6 video mode switches (PAGE2, HIRES, MIXED, TEXT, 80COL, ALTCHAR) 1 cursor flash state (FLASHON) 4 language card banking switches (BANK1, PREWRITE, HIGHREAD, HIGHWRITE) Constructors SystemStatusViewModel(ISystemStatusProvider) Initializes a new instance of the SystemStatusViewModel class. public SystemStatusViewModel(ISystemStatusProvider status) Parameters status ISystemStatusProvider System status provider supplying reactive status updates. Remarks Immediate Subscription: Unlike EmulatorStateViewModel, this view model subscribes immediately to the status stream without using WhenActivated. This ensures system status is always up-to-date and available for display. Initialization: After subscribing to the stream, applies the current snapshot from Current to initialize all properties with correct starting values. This prevents a brief period where properties would be default (false) until the first update arrives. Update Frequency: System status updates are relatively infrequent (typically triggered by soft switch changes or periodic polling), so immediate subscription has minimal performance impact compared to the frequently-updating EmulatorState. Properties State80Store Gets the state of the 80STORE soft switch. public bool State80Store { get; } Property Value bool True when 80STORE is active, false otherwise. Remarks When active, the PAGE2 switch controls auxiliary memory access rather than display page selection. Affects memory mapping for $0400-$07FF and $2000-$3FFF ranges. StateAltCharSet Gets the state of the ALTCHAR soft switch. public bool StateAltCharSet { get; } Property Value bool True when using alternate character set, false for primary character set. Remarks Selects between primary and alternate character sets in text mode. The alternate character set includes MouseText characters and inverse lowercase letters. StateAltZp Gets the state of the ALTZP soft switch. public bool StateAltZp { get; } Property Value bool True when using auxiliary zero page/stack, false when using main memory. Remarks When active, zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) are mapped to auxiliary RAM. Allows programs to maintain separate zero page/stack contexts. StateAnn0 Gets the state of annunciator 0. public bool StateAnn0 { get; } Property Value bool True when annunciator 0 is on, false when off. Remarks General-purpose output bit controlled by $C058/$C059. Often used for peripheral control or as a flag for custom hardware. StateAnn1 Gets the state of annunciator 1. public bool StateAnn1 { get; } Property Value bool True when annunciator 1 is on, false when off. Remarks General-purpose output bit controlled by $C05A/$C05B. Often used for peripheral control or as a flag for custom hardware. StateAnn2 Gets the state of annunciator 2. public bool StateAnn2 { get; } Property Value bool True when annunciator 2 is on, false when off. Remarks General-purpose output bit controlled by $C05C/$C05D. Often used for peripheral control or as a flag for custom hardware. StateAnn3 Gets the state of annunciator 3 (also known as DGR - Double Graphics). public bool StateAnn3 { get; } Property Value bool True when annunciator 3 is on, false when off. Remarks General-purpose output bit controlled by $C05E/$C05F. Commonly used to enable double hi-res graphics mode on the Apple IIe. Double Hi-Res Mode: When combined with other switches, enables 560x192 16-color graphics mode using both main and auxiliary memory. StateBank1 Gets the state of the BANK1 soft switch. public bool StateBank1 { get; } Property Value bool True when using language card bank 1, false when using bank 2. Remarks Controls which 4KB bank of RAM is mapped into $D000-$DFFF. Bank 2 is at lower addresses, bank 1 is at higher addresses in the physical RAM chip. StateFlashOn Gets the cursor/mode indicator flash state. public bool StateFlashOn { get; } Property Value bool True when flash is on (visible), false when flash is off (hidden). Remarks Toggles at approximately 2.1 Hz (~476ms period) to control cursor blinking and mode indicator flashing. Used for inverse/flashing characters in text mode. Usage: Characters with bit 6 set (flashing) alternate between normal and inverse video based on this state. StateHiRes Gets the state of the HIRES soft switch. public bool StateHiRes { get; } Property Value bool True for hi-res graphics mode, false for low-res graphics mode. Remarks Controls whether graphics mode uses hi-res (280x192 or 560x192) or low-res (40x48). Only affects graphics mode; has no effect when TEXT mode is active. StateHighRead Gets the HIGHREAD soft switch state. public bool StateHighRead { get; } Property Value bool True when reading from language card RAM, false when reading from ROM. Remarks Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF come from RAM or ROM. Independent of write enable. StateHighWrite Gets the HIGHWRITE soft switch state. public bool StateHighWrite { get; } Property Value bool True when writing to language card RAM is enabled, false when write-protected. Remarks Controls whether writes to $D000-$FFFF are allowed to modify RAM. Requires the two-access write enable sequence (PREWRITE) to activate. Write Enable Sequence: Two consecutive accesses to specific language card addresses are required to enable writing, preventing accidental ROM overwrites. StateIntCxRom Gets the state of the INTCXROM soft switch. public bool StateIntCxRom { get; } Property Value bool True when using internal ROM, false when using peripheral card ROMs. Remarks Controls whether accesses to $C100-$CFFF use internal ROM or peripheral card ROMs. When true (default at power-on), internal ROM is active; when false, slot ROMs are accessible. StateMixed Gets the state of the MIXED soft switch. public bool StateMixed { get; } Property Value bool True for mixed text/graphics mode, false for full-screen mode. Remarks When active, displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom (lines 20-23). Commonly used for games to show status information while displaying graphics. StatePage2 Gets the state of the PAGE2 soft switch. public bool StatePage2 { get; } Property Value bool True when displaying page 2, false when displaying page 1. Remarks Controls which video page is displayed (page 1 at $0400 or page 2 at $0800). Behavior is affected by 80STORE switch. When 80STORE is off: PAGE2 selects display page. When 80STORE is on: PAGE2 affects auxiliary memory access instead. StatePb0 Gets the state of pushbutton 0 (game controller button 0). public bool StatePb0 { get; } Property Value bool True when button 0 is pressed, false when released. Remarks Readable at $C061. Typically used for joystick or paddle button 0. StatePb1 Gets the state of pushbutton 1 (game controller button 1). public bool StatePb1 { get; } Property Value bool True when button 1 is pressed, false when released. Remarks Readable at $C062. Typically used for joystick or paddle button 1. StatePb2 Gets the state of pushbutton 2 (game controller button 2). public bool StatePb2 { get; } Property Value bool True when button 2 is pressed, false when released. Remarks Readable at $C063. Typically used for additional game controller buttons. StatePrewrite Gets the PREWRITE state (first step of write enable sequence). public bool StatePrewrite { get; } Property Value bool True when first access has occurred, false otherwise. Remarks First step in the two-access write enable sequence. When true, the next access to the appropriate language card address will enable writing (set HIGHWRITE). Implementation Note: This is an internal state flag, not directly controlled by a specific soft switch address. It's set by the first of two required accesses to enable language card RAM writing. StateRamRd Gets the state of the RAMRD soft switch. public bool StateRamRd { get; } Property Value bool True when reading from auxiliary RAM, false when reading from main RAM. Remarks Controls whether reads from certain memory ranges ($0200-$BFFF) come from main or auxiliary RAM. Typically used with 80-column card or extended memory applications. StateRamWrt Gets the state of the RAMWRT soft switch. public bool StateRamWrt { get; } Property Value bool True when writing to auxiliary RAM, false when writing to main RAM. Remarks Controls whether writes to certain memory ranges ($0200-$BFFF) go to main or auxiliary RAM. Can be different from RAMRD, allowing read-from-one/write-to-other configurations. StateShow80Col Gets the state of the 80COL (80VID) soft switch. public bool StateShow80Col { get; } Property Value bool True for 80-column display, false for 40-column display. Remarks When active, enables 80-column text mode using both main and auxiliary video memory. Requires auxiliary memory (80-column card or equivalent). StateSlotC3Rom Gets the state of the SLOTC3ROM soft switch. public bool StateSlotC3Rom { get; } Property Value bool True when using slot 3 ROM, false when using internal ROM. Remarks Controls whether accesses to $C300-$C3FF use internal ROM or slot 3 peripheral ROM. Independent of INTCXROM, allowing slot 3 to be controlled separately. StateTextMode Gets the state of the TEXT soft switch. public bool StateTextMode { get; } Property Value bool True for text mode, false for graphics mode. Remarks Master switch controlling text vs graphics display. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays graphics (low-res or hi-res based on HIRES switch).
